This is accomplished through a focus on external and internal research, culminating in a strategic social plan for a client organization.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Sports Public Relations
Description: Provides students with an overview of the rapidly developing sport industry from a public relations and strategic communications perspective.
Description_HTML: Provides students with an overview of the rapidly developing sport industry from a public relations and strategic communications perspective.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Sustainability Public Relations
Description: All future communication positions will involve sustainability-related activities, tasks, and challenges. In this course, we will discuss fundamental sustainability concepts and perspectives necessary to understand to effectively engage in sustainability communication and to help facilitating and communicating sustainability transformations.
Description_HTML: All future communication positions will involve sustainability-related activities, tasks, and challenges. In this course, we will discuss fundamental sustainability concepts and perspectives necessary to understand to effectively engage in sustainability communication and to help facilitating and communicating sustainability transformations.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Gamification and the Media
Description: Studies gamification (the use of game elements in non-game contexts) to help students analyze, critique, and make use of game elements in their own media production.
Requisites: Prereq: JCOM 280 or JCOM 311 or J 249.
Description_HTML:
Studies gamification (the use of game elements in non-game contexts) to help students analyze, critique, and make use of game elements in their own media production.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Making the Virtual a Reality
Description: Introduces students to immersive media’s rich foundational scholarship and arms students with theoretical and practical tools to assess trends like platformization, digital surveillance and algorithmic bias.
Requisites: Prereq: One from JCOM 280, JCOM 311, J 201, J 249.

Introduces students to immersive media’s rich foundational scholarship and arms students with theoretical and practical tools to assess trends like platformization, digital surveillance and algorithmic bias.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Analog Games
Description: Analyzes analog games through genre-based modules (e.g., cards, dice, board games, etc.) that evaluate key issues in game history, mechanics and design, and social impacts.
Requisites: Prereq: One from JCOM 280, JCOM 311, J 249.

Analyzes analog games through genre-based modules (e.g., cards, dice, board games, etc.) that evaluate key issues in game history, mechanics and design, and social impacts.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Science Communication and Decision Making
Description: This course explores the specific areas of research that inform strategic communication to the public about science, health, and the environment and the public's engagement with those fields. Building on readings in areas such as decision science, numeracy, and health and environmental communication, students will be asked to think carefully about decisions and judgments that they and others make, and how strategic communication can harness decision making so that complex science is useful in improving individual and societal well-being for a variety of audiences.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Environmental Communication Strategies
Description: Environmental Communication Strategies explores how public relations and advertising have played a role in the public understanding of major environmental issues. In addition, a portion of this course will be focused on interpersonal communication and developing approaches to constructive climate conversations.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Eco and Social Justice Communication
Description: This class is designed to introduce students to environmental justice and social change communications. Students will spend time understanding how environmental and social justice has been communicated and strategies for achieving a more justice-oriented world through improved and accessible communications. Students will learn to evaluate environmental justice communication strategies and develop their own communications for various media platforms.

Subject: JCOM
Title: US Film Industry

Subject: JCOM
Title: Internet Law and Regulation
Description: This is a survey course on Internet Law and Regulation. It is designed to ground students in a variety of internet-related laws and policies they will likely confront in their professional careers and personal lives, including an overview of the legal and regulatory frameworks governing communication and commercial activities conducted via the internet and explorations into topics such as online platforms, free speech online, privacy and surveillance, electronic contracts, domain names, copyrights, trademarks, and computer crime.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Data, Media, Surveillance
Description: Data/Media/Surveillance is a discussion-intensive and project-based course designed to expose students to a range of issues at the intersections of data, information, media, and surveillance in society and culture. Students will develop the analytical tools to explore (through conducting research and/or producing creative projects and developing normative arguments) how surveillance, commercial and government data processing practices, and media platforms and technologies are at play in our modern world.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Media Technologies and Structures: [Topic]
Description: Examine how emerging communication technologies and contemporary media shape and produce new forms of social organization, behavior, and cultural production. Explore how media infrastructures and regulatory environments influence discourse, democracy, and public life.
Repeatable: Repeatable 3 times for a maximum of 16 credits when topic changes

Subject: JCOM
Title: Culture, Power, Media: [Topic]
Description: Explores issues of culture, identity, and power, including the role media play in reinforcing social, political, and economic disparities. Majors and minors only. Repeatable three times for a maximum of 16 credits when topic changes.
Repeatable: Repeatable 3 times for a maximum of 16 credits when topic changes

Subject: JCOM
Title: Cross Cultural Monster Narratives
Description: This course explores the cross-cultural narratives surrounding dominant monster archetypes. To identify the similarities and differences between monster stories and traditions, students will consider how each unit’s monster has been embraced, remixed, or subverted by different groups through different media at different moments in time, with a particular focus on the dynamics of race, gender, sex, and power that manifest through/as the monster.
Description_HTML: This course explores the cross-cultural narratives surrounding dominant monster archetypes. To identify the similarities and differences between monster stories and traditions, students will consider how each unit’s monster has been embraced, remixed, or subverted by different groups through different media at different moments in time, with a particular focus on the dynamics of race, gender, sex, and power that manifest through/as the monster.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Global Television
Description: This class will introduce television content from various parts of the globe – Asia, Africa, Europe and the Middle East to demonstrate how social and political conditions shape the formation of popular culture. Throughout the term, students will analyze specific shows from each region to come to a better understanding of global television as a whole.
Description_HTML: This class will introduce television content from various parts of the globe – Asia, Africa, Europe and the Middle East to demonstrate how social and political conditions shape the formation of popular culture. Throughout the term, students will analyze specific shows from each region to come to a better understanding of global television as a whole.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Reality Television
Description: This course focuses on a popular television genre – reality television – and uses it as a means to explore key issues in contemporary culture and society.
Description_HTML: This course focuses on a popular television genre – reality television – and uses it as a means to explore key issues in contemporary culture and society.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Documentary Pre-Production
Description: Students learn to research, plan, budget for, and develop a documentary film idea. They gain experience shooting a sizzle and pitching projects to potential producers. Several documentary forms will be explored, including portraits, ethnographies, interviews, personal stories, processes and events, and re-enactments.
Description_HTML: Students learn to research, plan, budget for, and develop a documentary film idea. They gain experience shooting a sizzle and pitching projects to potential producers. Several documentary forms will be explored, including portraits, ethnographies, interviews, personal stories, processes and events, and re-enactments.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Documentary Production
Description: Get experience shooting a short documentary worthy of broadcast screening, film festival exhibition, or another venue.
Description_HTML:
Get experience shooting a short documentary worthy of broadcast screening, film festival exhibition, or another venue.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Documentary Post-Production
Description: Trains students to edit and do post-production work on their documentary film projects.
Description_HTML:
Trains students to edit and do post-production work on their documentary film projects.
Requisites: Prereq: JCOM 521 or J 521.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Latin American Cinema
Description: This is a survey course, exploring the film industry of various regions of Latin America (North, Central and South America, including the Caribbean). The course will expose students to classic and contemporary films, including Latin American Golden Age cinema, cinema movements like Brazilian Cinema Novo, the militant film era of the 1960s through 1980s, and films created for global distribution at the end of the 20th century to the present.
Description_HTML: This is a survey course, exploring the film industry of various regions of Latin America (North, Central and South America, including the Caribbean). The course will expose students to classic and contemporary films, including Latin American Golden Age cinema, cinema movements like Brazilian Cinema Novo, the militant film era of the 1960s through 1980s, and films created for global distribution at the end of the 20th century to the present.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Latino Roots I
Description: Documents Latino history in the racial history of what is now Oregon since 1500 and teaches students to conduct oral history interviews. Multilisted with ANTH 527M. Sequence with JCOM 528M. Offered alternate years.
Description_HTML: Documents Latino history in the racial history of what is now Oregon since 1500 and teaches students to conduct oral history interviews. Multilisted with ANTH 527M. Sequence with JCOM 528M. Offered alternate years.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Latino Roots II
Description: Continuation of Latino Roots I, designed for producing a short documentary using oral history as the story. Covers basic theory and practice of digital film-video documentary production. Multilisted with ANTH 528M. Sequence with JCOM 527M. Offered alternate years.
Description_HTML:
Continuation of Latino Roots I, designed for producing a short documentary using oral history as the story. Covers basic theory and practice of digital film-video documentary production. Multilisted with ANTH 528M. Sequence with JCOM 527M. Offered alternate years.

Subject: JCOM
Title: In-Depth Reporting: [Topic]
Description: In-Depth Reporting courses offer experience in deeper exploration of timely themes important to the journalist.
Description_HTML: In-Depth Reporting courses offer experience in deeper exploration of timely themes important to the journalist.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Data Journalism
Description: This course underscores and advances the power to identify, locate, obtain and analyze data is central to journalism’s public service role and the capacity of journalism to engage a wider audience.
Description_HTML: This course underscores and advances the power to identify, locate, obtain and analyze data is central to journalism’s public service role and the capacity of journalism to engage a wider audience.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Solutions Journalism
Description: What is the role of journalism and the journalist in a democratic society? It’s a provocative question at a time when the journalism field is taking a hard look at how best to incorporate its past into its future. This course seeks to answer this by doing journalism that includes offering well-researched solution possibilities to social problems.
Description_HTML: What is the role of journalism and the journalist in a democratic society? It’s a provocative question at a time when the journalism field is taking a hard look at how best to incorporate its past into its future. This course seeks to answer this by doing journalism that includes offering well-researched solution possibilities to social problems.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Catalyst Journalism
Description: This course teaches a pioneering approach to journalism developed here at the SOJC, one that seeks impact by combining investigative reporting with solutions journalism, an emerging evidence-based reporting strategy that highlights social progress and innovation.
Description_HTML: This course teaches a pioneering approach to journalism developed here at the SOJC, one that seeks impact by combining investigative reporting with solutions journalism, an emerging evidence-based reporting strategy that highlights social progress and innovation.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Investigative Journalism
Description: This class challenges students to do the important work through thorough investigative journalism using in-depth interviews, public records, and mastery of story. Students are expected to publish their vetted work at the end of the term.
Description_HTML: This class challenges students to do the important work through thorough investigative journalism using in-depth interviews, public records, and mastery of story. Students are expected to publish their vetted work at the end of the term.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Profiles and Narrative
Description: This course provides you with the essential skills, methods and mindset to produce compelling narratives and in-depth, insightful profiles that will engage with compelling stories about the human experience.
Description_HTML: This course provides you with the essential skills, methods and mindset to produce compelling narratives and in-depth, insightful profiles that will engage with compelling stories about the human experience.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Magazine Production: [Topic]
Description: In JCOM 436, Topics in Magazine, you will use skills developed in other journalism courses, such as interviewing, reporting and photography, to produce feature stories of the highest quality so that they can be published in print and digitally. Your work will tell the stories of individuals and issues relevant to local and regional audiences.
Repeatable: Repeatable 3 times for a maximum of 16 credits when topic changes

Subject: JCOM
Title: Science Story
Description: This class will ground students in a systematic and rigorous pitching, reporting, and editing process to create work for professional publication. Students work independently and collaboratively to create a publication and develop advanced portfolio work. Throughout, students will constantly justify their decisions based on how to best present environmental, scientific, and policy information in ways that are accurate, clear and accessible to a general audience.
Repeatable: Repeatable 1 time for a maximum of 8 credits
Description_HTML:

Subject: JCOM
Title: Sports Story
Description: This class will instruct students in the fundaments of sports reporting, including how to interview athletes and sports executives; cover sporting events; and develop sourcing for investigative and enterprise sports feature writing. Students will attend live events, honing their skills as breaking news reporters. They will also work on longer-form pieces that will allow them to practice skills in profile, narrative, trend and enterprise reporting. Repeatable three times for a maximum of 16 credits.
Repeatable: Repeatable 3 times for a maximum of 16 credits

Subject: JCOM
Title: Audio Journalism II Narrative and Podcasting Capstone
Description: This class will enhance students' scriptwriting and narrative structuring skills, manage complex audio projects, understand podcast monetization, and master performance and sound design. They will also innovate in audio storytelling formats and gain expertise in producing Q&A style podcasts. Assignments include developing a multi-episode podcast or documentary, creating a chatcast, and devising a business and advertising plan for a podcast series, focusing on narrative impact, technical proficiency, and market viability.

Assignments include developing a multi-episode podcast or documentary, creating a chatcast, and devising a business and advertising plan for a podcast series, focusing on narrative impact, technical proficiency, and market viability.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Video Journalism III Broadcast Capstone
Description: Students will work as a team to produce a live broadcast each week and master what it takes to be a professional broadcaster. Students will also build a portfolio. Shows will be varied in type (including but not limited to traditional newscast, sportscast, morning show, longform news magazine) so that students have a wide-ranging experience base.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Photojournalism II Photo Story
Description: Photojournalism II Photo Story, you will develop the skills to tell complex stories using the still image, using the framework of best journalistic practices. The course emphasizes photo skills to tell compelling stories found in the community.

Title: Visual Production: [Topic]

Subject: JCOM
Title: Brand Strategy
Description: This course emphasizes the importance and challenge of compelling creative brand strategy, including how strategy impacts creative work, internal agency partnerships, and, importantly, business success.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Brands and Sustainability
Description: This course explores the positioning and actionable strategies for building and maintaining sustainable brands.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Sports Brand Strategy
Description: Bridges concepts of business and creative strategy to understand the process of building compelling brands in the context of sport. Representing a multi-billion-dollar industry, sport provides a unique platform to study how strong brands are developed, positioned, and brought to life in creative ways.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Advertising Campaigns
Description: Seniors work in teams to produce a comprehensive campaign involving every aspect of advertising, ranging from market research through creative and media strategy formulation to execution. The collaborative teamwork is based on expertise and practice in industry. Journalism: advertising majors only.
Description_HTML: Seniors work in teams to produce a comprehensive campaign involving every aspect of advertising, ranging from market research through creative and media strategy formulation to execution. Subject: JCOM
Title: National Student Advertising Competition Campaigns
Description: Dedicated teamwork focusing on the annual American Advertising Federation National Student Advertising Competition (NSAC). Entrance is by portfolio and interview application. Students work a full year on the competition and resulting campaign book. This course is the focal point of that work.

Title: Art Direction and Design Studio

Subject: JCOM
Title: Strategic Communications Research Methods
Description: This course is designed to provide you with an understanding of common research methods used in public relations and advertising when developing, implementing, and evaluating a strategic campaign. The class emphasizes real world applications.

Subject: JCOM
Title: The Public Relations Planning Process
Description: In this course, students will learn public relations campaign planning and administration, crisis communication and issues management, applied research, writing objectives and tactics, evaluation methods, and constructing budgets and timelines. While students do not execute a campaign in this class, it equips them with the process to be able to execute effectively in the capstone PR Campaigns class ( JCOM 572 ).

Subject: JCOM
Title: Public Relations Campaigns
Description: Public Relations Campaigns is the capstone course in the sequence of instruction offered in public relations. It provides students with an opportunity to apply the fundamentals of what they have learned over their course of study to actual client work and business problems. The course is designed to build professional experience and prepare you for the world that awaits you after graduation.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Public Relations Strategies: [Topic]

Students can expect to engage in discussions and assignments about current Public Relations trends, career preparation, and Public Relations case studies. Example topics include professional development, strategic health communication, and strategic non-profit communication.
Repeatable: Repeatable 3 times for a maximum of 16 credits when topic changes

Subject: JCOM
Title: Crisis Communication
Description: Covers crisis planning, communications and issues management with an emphasis on real-world application and case study analysis of sudden events and crisis. Classes will include lecture, online and in-class discussion and student presentations. Students will also participate in media training for crisis situations and a real-time online scenario.

Subject: JCOM
Title: New Technology and Public Relations
Description: New and emerging technology for Public Relations and communications professionals provides an overview of the latest technologies communicators are using to reach their audiences. Participants will have the opportunity to experience and study PR use cases for Augmented Reality (AR), Virtual Reality (VR), Extended Reality (XR), Mixed Reality (MR) and Artificial Intelligence (AI).

Subject: JCOM
Title: Social Media Strategies
Description: This course focuses on social media research, analysis and planning. It is designed to build on students’ existing foundation of the “how to” of specifics tools (how to publish content) and to help students understand the why and when of social media for the purpose of building relationships and creating conversations with stakeholders and key audiences. This is accomplished through a focus on external and internal research, culminating in a strategic social plan for a client organization.

Title: Sports Public Relations

Title: Sustainability Public Relations
Description: All future communication positions will involve sustainability-related activities, tasks, and challenges. In this course, we will discuss fundamental sustainability concepts and perspectives necessary to understand to effectively engage in sustainability communication and to help facilitating and communicating sustainability transformations.

Title: Science Communication and Decision Making
Description: This course explores the specific areas of research that inform strategic communication to the public about science, health, and the environment and the public's engagement with those fields. Subject: JCOM
Title: Media and Society
Description: Overview of the features of the contemporary media environment, its historical development, and the impacts of media on culture and society.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Media Theory I
Description: First in a three-part sequence introducing students to media theory, focusing on the social scientific tradition. Sequence with JCOM 613, JCOM 614.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Teaching and the Professional Life
Description: Explores teaching strategies, curriculum development, and other aspects of academic professional life in journalism and communication.
Description_HTML: Explores teaching strategies, curriculum development, and other aspects of academic professional life in journalism and communication.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Introduction to Strategic Communication Marketing
Description: Discussion of fundamental marketing concepts from the perspective of the manager. Analysis of complex marketing challenges in research, segmentation, targeting, pricing, distribution, and branding.
Description_HTML: Discussion of fundamental marketing concepts from the perspective of the manager. Analysis of complex marketing challenges in research, segmentation, targeting, pricing, distribution, and branding.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Communication Theory and Practice: [Topic]
Description: Theory, research, and practice of strategic communication. Topics may include relationship management, risk communication, identity and culture, and social media theory.
Repeatable: Repeatable 4 times for a maximum of 20 credits
Description_HTML:
Theory, research, and practice of strategic communication. Topics may include relationship management, risk communication, identity and culture, and social media theory.
Repeatable 4 times for a maximum of 20 credits

Subject: JCOM
Title: Strategic Communication Leadership
Description: Elements of managing and leading organizations; examination of key issues faced by leaders. Topics include leadership theory, leading change, dealing with conflict, and performance and strategic management.
Description_HTML: Elements of managing and leading organizations; examination of key issues faced by leaders. Topics include leadership theory, leading change, dealing with conflict, and performance and strategic management.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Foundations of Strategic Communication
Description: Reviews major theories, models, and practices in strategic communications. Topics include media effects and persuasion as applied to public relations, advertising, and other strategic communication.
Description_HTML: Reviews major theories, models, and practices in strategic communications. Topics include media effects and persuasion as applied to public relations, advertising, and other strategic communication.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Campaign Planning and Management
Description: Explores strategic tools and creativity to develop effective communications campaigns that address real-world challenges. Sequence with JCOM 623.
Description_HTML: Explores strategic tools and creativity to develop effective communications campaigns that address real-world challenges. Sequence with JCOM 623.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Creativity in Strategic Communication
Description: Explores the use of creative conceptual thinking as part of the strategic basis in successful communication campaigns, culminating with student teams focused on a client project. Sequence with JCOM 622.
Description_HTML: Explores the use of creative conceptual thinking as part of the strategic basis in successful communication campaigns, culminating with student teams focused on a client project. Sequence with JCOM 622.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Strategic Communication: [Topic]
Description: Explores problems and specialized skills needed in strategic communication management. Examples include crisis communication, creativity in business, corporate social responsibility.
Repeatable: Repeatable 5 times for a maximum of 12 credits when topic changes
Description_HTML:
Explores problems and specialized skills needed in strategic communication management. Examples include crisis communication, creativity in business, corporate social responsibility.
Repeatable 5 times for a maximum of 12 credits when topic changes

Subject: JCOM
Title: Finance for Strategic Communication
Description: This course focuses on the core basics of the Income Statement for professionals working in public relations, advertising, marketing, corporate communication or other related fields.
Description_HTML: This course focuses on the core basics of the Income Statement for professionals working in public relations, advertising, marketing, corporate communication or other related fields.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Video Production I
Description: This is an introductory video storytelling course for incoming Multimedia Journalism master’s students who enter the program with little or no hands-on video production experience. This course will serve as a foundation of theory and technique, with an emphasis on the technical aspects of video production and editing. Production techniques will be introduced through a series of content production exercises. We will also explore video production through the examination of outstanding web-based media. We will learn the vocabulary specific to multimedia production that we will incorporate into class discussions and written analysis.
Description_HTML: This is an introductory video storytelling course for incoming Multimedia Journalism master’s students who enter the program with little or no hands-on video production experience. This course will serve as a foundation of theory and technique, with an emphasis on the technical aspects of video production and editing. Production techniques will be introduced through a series of content production exercises. We will also explore video production through the examination of outstanding web-based media. We will learn the vocabulary specific to multimedia production that we will incorporate into class discussions and written analysis.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Video Production II
Description: This is the video production course for incoming Multimedia Journalism master’s students. Students learn basics in telling a cohesive visual story that combines interview with storytelling cinema vérité footage (B-roll).
Description_HTML: This is the video production course for incoming Multimedia Journalism master’s students. Students learn basics in telling a cohesive visual story that combines interview with storytelling cinema vérité footage (B-roll).

Subject: JCOM
Title: Media and Communication Ethics: [Topic]
Description: This course explores ethical issues facing media workers and media users in culture and society today. Topics may include digital ethics, strategic communication ethics, visual ethics and global media ethics.
Repeatable: Repeatable 1 time for a maximum of 8 credits
Description_HTML:
This course explores ethical issues facing media workers and media users in culture and society today. Topics may include digital ethics, strategic communication ethics, visual ethics and global media ethics.
Repeatable 1 time for a maximum of 8 credits

Subject: JCOM
Title: Multimedia Production: [Topic]
Description: 2-credit technical workshops that focus on foundational, up-to-date multimedia production skills. Examples include: video production, audio production, still photography, studio lighting, animation, drone cinematography. Each workshop typically includes lessons in related software such as Adobe Premiere, Audition, After Effects, Photoshop and DaVinci Resolve.
Repeatable: Repeatable 4 times for a maximum of 10 credits when topic changes
Description_HTML:
2-credit technical workshops that focus on foundational, up-to-date multimedia production skills. Examples include: video production, audio production, still photography, studio lighting, animation, drone cinematography. Each workshop typically includes lessons in related software such as Adobe Premiere, Audition, After Effects, Photoshop and DaVinci Resolve.
Repeatable 4 times for a maximum of 10 credits when topic changes

Subject: JCOM
Title: Foundations of Multimedia Journalism
Description: Serves as a foundation of theory and technique, with an introduction to storytelling forms, technical production skills, and the visual language. Students will learn how to use the tools of the trade so that they can communicate effectively with other multimedia journalists.
Description_HTML: Serves as a foundation of theory and technique, with an introduction to storytelling forms, technical production skills, and the visual language. Students will learn how to use the tools of the trade so that they can communicate effectively with other multimedia journalists.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Story Production
Description: Building upon the lessons from Thinking Story and Video II, students refine production skills, elevate their storytelling, and enhance their proficiency working with the visual language. Students strive to create a short documentary video project worthy of inclusion in their professional portfolio. Distribution and approaches to social media are also covered.
Description_HTML:
Building upon the lessons from Thinking Story and Video II, students refine production skills, elevate their storytelling, and enhance their proficiency working with the visual language. Students strive to create a short documentary video project worthy of inclusion in their professional portfolio. Distribution and approaches to social media are also covered.
Requisites: Prereq: JCOM 628, JCOM 635.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Storytelling Capstone
Description: This is the capstone project class for the Multimedia Journalism program. Students work collaboratively to create a high-impact project that combines lessons from Thinking Story, Community Listening and all previous production courses.
Description_HTML: This is the capstone project class for the Multimedia Journalism program. Students work collaboratively to create a high-impact project that combines lessons from Thinking Story, Community Listening and all previous production courses.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Reporting within Communities
Description: Students explore and practice emerging "community-first" concepts of journalism and reporting to identify the needs of the communities served, co-designing processes and solutions to keep them engaged.
Description_HTML: Students explore and practice emerging "community-first" concepts of journalism and reporting to identify the needs of the communities served, co-designing processes and solutions to keep them engaged.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Thinking Story
Description: Recognize and use fundamental approaches to narrative storytelling to create dynamic and engaging multimedia projects.
Description_HTML: Recognize and use fundamental approaches to narrative storytelling to create dynamic and engaging multimedia projects.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Pitching and Treatment
Description: This 5-week workshop is designed to help students successfully launch their Terminal Project. Drawing on story research skills from previous classes, students learn how to create professional pitch decks, treatments and budgets in order to market their projects. These tools are applicable for terminal projects involving documentary video, podcasting, books, articles, photography or Virtual Reality.
Description_HTML:
This 5-week workshop is designed to help students successfully launch their Terminal Project. Drawing on story research skills from previous classes, students learn how to create professional pitch decks, treatments and budgets in order to market their projects. These tools are applicable for terminal projects involving documentary video, podcasting, books, articles, photography or Virtual Reality.
Requisites: Prereq: JCOM 632, JCOM 656.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Community Listening
Description: This interdisciplinary course invites students to delve into community listening as a foundational approach to effective storytelling, journalism, and public engagement. The course emphasizes the importance of values, ethics, and participatory methods in creating and disseminating community narratives. Students will learn to apply these principles to enhance public engagement, foster community trust, and support local civic information ecosystems through empathetic listening and inclusive storytelling.
Description_HTML: This interdisciplinary course invites students to delve into community listening as a foundational approach to effective storytelling, journalism, and public engagement. The course emphasizes the importance of values, ethics, and participatory methods in creating and disseminating community narratives. Students will learn to apply these principles to enhance public engagement, foster community trust, and support local civic information ecosystems through empathetic listening and inclusive storytelling.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Community Voices
Description: "Community Voices: Harnessing Local Stories into a Podcast" is a four-credit course where students learn to listen deeply, facilitate meaningful community discussions, and create a participatory-based audio episode. The course focuses on developing skills in community listening, understanding diverse perspectives, and fostering dialogues that build trust and civic cohesion. By the end of the course, students will produce a podcast story that embodies the voices and stories of the community they engage with.
Description_HTML: "Community Voices: Harnessing Local Stories into a Podcast" is a four-credit course where students learn to listen deeply, facilitate meaningful community discussions, and create a participatory-based audio episode. The course focuses on developing skills in community listening, understanding diverse perspectives, and fostering dialogues that build trust and civic cohesion. By the end of the course, students will produce a podcast story that embodies the voices and stories of the community they engage with.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Foundations of Explanatory Video Journalism
Description: Students explore and practice concepts in visual explanation and explanatory video.
Description_HTML: Students explore and practice concepts in visual explanation and explanatory video.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Qualitative Research Methods
Description: Introduces qualitative research methods, including traditional historical inquiry, oral history, ethnography, and participant observation.
Description_HTML: Introduces qualitative research methods, including traditional historical inquiry, oral history, ethnography, and participant observation.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Quantitative Research Methods
Description: Introduces and analyzes quantitative research methods in terms of design, measurement, inference, and validity. Focuses on conceptualization in communication research.
Description_HTML: Introduces and analyzes quantitative research methods in terms of design, measurement, inference, and validity. Focuses on conceptualization in communication research.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Advanced Doctoral Research
Description: This course prepares students to develop, write, and propose research, with attention to integrating theory and method in the preparation of a dissertation proposal for the Communication and Media Studies Doctoral Program.
Description_HTML:
This course prepares students to develop, write, and propose research, with attention to integrating theory and method in the preparation of a dissertation proposal for the Communication and Media Studies Doctoral Program.
Requisites: Prereq: JCOM 641, JCOM 642.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Philosophy of Communication
Description: Explores the philosophical foundations of communication in the United States, including political philosophies that range from Milton to McLuhan.
Description_HTML: Explores the philosophical foundations of communication in the United States, including political philosophies that range from Milton to McLuhan.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Political Economy of Communication
Description: Introduction to the political economy of communication. Includes such issues as ownership and control patterns; the role of the state; labor; intellectual property rights; and international markets.
Description_HTML: Introduction to the political economy of communication. Includes such issues as ownership and control patterns; the role of the state; labor; intellectual property rights; and international markets.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Cultural Approaches to Communication
Description: Examination of communication and mediated communication as cultural processes in the production and reproduction of social systems.
Description_HTML: Examination of communication and mediated communication as cultural processes in the production and reproduction of social systems.

Subject: JCOM
Title: International Communication
Description: Examines global communication structures and processes and their consequences. Topics include new technologies, news and information organizations, cross-cultural uses of Western media, and information policies.
Description_HTML: Examines global communication structures and processes and their consequences. Topics include new technologies, news and information organizations, cross-cultural uses of Western media, and information policies.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Fundamentals of Reporting and Writing
Description: Journalism requires strong writing, and strong writing begins with strong reporting. This is the foundational class for success in the Journalism Master's Program. This class is designed to teach the fundamental skills required to be a journalist: research, interviewing, analysis and writing and will provide the foundation for success in the Journalism Master's program. An intensive boot-camp experience that will immediately immerse you in the world of journalism. Very much a hands-on experience, students will practice the skills they learn and discuss in class including interviewing, writing and rewriting.
Description_HTML: Journalism requires strong writing, and strong writing begins with strong reporting. This is the foundational class for success in the Journalism Master's Program. This class is designed to teach the fundamental skills required to be a journalist: research, interviewing, analysis and writing and will provide the foundation for success in the Journalism Master's program. An intensive boot-camp experience that will immediately immerse you in the world of journalism. Very much a hands-on experience, students will practice the skills they learn and discuss in class including interviewing, writing and rewriting.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Multimedia Fundamentals
Description: Journalism requires skills beyond writing. This class will give students basic skills in audio, photography and video reporting through lectures and hands-on assignments. This is a foundational class for success in the Journalism Master's Program. An intensive boot-camp experience that will provide you with a basic understanding of skills beyond writing. You will learn to tell stories using tools beyond the written word and gain a basic understanding of video and audio production.
Description_HTML: Journalism requires skills beyond writing. This class will give students basic skills in audio, photography and video reporting through lectures and hands-on assignments. This is a foundational class for success in the Journalism Master's Program. An intensive boot-camp experience that will provide you with a basic understanding of skills beyond writing. You will learn to tell stories using tools beyond the written word and gain a basic understanding of video and audio production.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Public Affairs Journalism
Description: This course will elevate the quality of your journalism by broadening your reporting experiences, strengthening your news-gathering skills and giving you tools to sharpen your writing. In this course, you will pursue journalism’s ideal of public service while meeting the real-world demands of reporting timely, consequential and ethical stories. You’ll tackle issues in our community beyond campus to gain experience on par with professional journalists.
Description_HTML:
This course will elevate the quality of your journalism by broadening your reporting experiences, strengthening your news-gathering skills and giving you tools to sharpen your writing. In this course, you will pursue journalism’s ideal of public service while meeting the real-world demands of reporting timely, consequential and ethical stories. You’ll tackle issues in our community beyond campus to gain experience on par with professional journalists.
Requisites: Prereq: JCOM 651.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Journalism Rights and Responsibilities
Description: Journalists need to understand their rights to report, which starts in the U.S. with the First Amendment, and their responsibilities as ethical journalists and human beings. This course explores the ethical and legal considerations that shape the field of professional journalism in the United States.
Description_HTML:
Journalists need to understand their rights to report, which starts in the U.S. with the First Amendment, and their responsibilities as ethical journalists and human beings. This course explores the ethical and legal considerations that shape the field of professional journalism in the United States.
Requisites: Prereq: JCOM 653.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Law and Business for Media
Description: This course introduces students to the fundamentals of producing independent documentary film and multimedia journalism, from story development to distribution. Along the way, you’ll learn concepts that will help you run a business either as a documentary filmmaker or journalist, including pitching and budgeting a project, securing legal rights, building the right team for the story and finding a distributor or publisher. All of this in service of supporting and making excellent nonfiction media that can help shape the world.
Description_HTML: This course introduces students to the fundamentals of producing independent documentary film and multimedia journalism, from story development to distribution. Along the way, you’ll learn concepts that will help you run a business either as a documentary filmmaker or journalist, including pitching and budgeting a project, securing legal rights, building the right team for the story and finding a distributor or publisher. All of this in service of supporting and making excellent nonfiction media that can help shape the world.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Advanced Research Methods: [Topic]
Description: Explores specific qualitative or quantitative communication research methods. Topics may include discourse analysis, oral history, historical methods, legal methods, content analysis, and survey methods.
Description_HTML:
Explores specific qualitative or quantitative communication research methods. Topics may include discourse analysis, oral history, historical methods, legal methods, content analysis, and survey methods.
Repeatable 99 times when topic changes

Subject: JCOM
Title: Foundations of Strategic Sport Communication
Description: Presents and reviews major theories, models, and practices in sports communication. Theoretical topics include sports media effects and persuasion as applied to broadcast, public relations, advertising, and other strategic communication. Cultural, societal and industry relevance also discussed.
Description_HTML: Presents and reviews major theories, models, and practices in sports communication. Theoretical topics include sports media effects and persuasion as applied to broadcast, public relations, advertising, and other strategic communication. Cultural, societal and industry relevance also discussed.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Foundations in Immersive Media
Description: In this course, students will explore the uses of social virtual worlds as well as augmented and virtual reality through the strategic lens of communications (marketing, advertising, branding) and community building. Students will investigate the attributes of the technologies that create both opportunities and barriers to successful communication. Students will critically examine current case examples to determine possible use cases. Ultimately, students will develop a proposal for an issue or organization that uses mediated reality to engage audiences for strategic outcomes, recognizing limitations with recommendations to overcome those limitations.
Description_HTML: In this course, students will explore the uses of social virtual worlds as well as augmented and virtual reality through the strategic lens of communications (marketing, advertising, branding) and community building. Students will investigate the attributes of the technologies that create both opportunities and barriers to successful communication. Students will critically examine current case examples to determine possible use cases. Ultimately, students will develop a proposal for an issue or organization that uses mediated reality to engage audiences for strategic outcomes, recognizing limitations with recommendations to overcome those limitations.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Immersive Media Psychology
Description: This course offers an overview of media psychology with an emphasis on emerging media platforms. Students learn why humans consume certain types of media content and gain an understanding of the affective, behavioral, and cognitive implications of media messages in order to better understand audiences.
Description_HTML: This course offers an overview of media psychology with an emphasis on emerging media platforms. Students learn why humans consume certain types of media content and gain an understanding of the affective, behavioral, and cognitive implications of media messages in order to better understand audiences.

Subject: JCOM
Title: User Experience Design
Description: This 10-week course is designed to teach fundamental principles of user experience (UX) and human-centered design (HCD) in the context of strategic communication. By design, this course is heavily interdisciplinary in nature, relying on various theoretical and applied approaches drawn from fields of human-computer interaction (HCI), media psychology, and many others. UX is also context-specific, and we will be highlighting UX design in various domains, including mobile apps, traditional web, service and customer experience, gaming, and product design, among others.
Description_HTML: This 10-week course is designed to teach fundamental principles of user experience (UX) and human-centered design (HCD) in the context of strategic communication. By design, this course is heavily interdisciplinary in nature, relying on various theoretical and applied approaches drawn from fields of human-computer interaction (HCI), media psychology, and many others. UX is also context-specific, and we will be highlighting UX design in various domains, including mobile apps, traditional web, service and customer experience, gaming, and product design, among others.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Immersive Marketing in Communication
Description: This course was created to help students begin to put immersive media in the context of marketing communications. It helps them begin to put strategy into the framework of immersive media as platforms for advertising, events, sales, and community building. Learn to develop a framework for building immersive media marketing strategies. Specific topics include leveraging augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), extended reality (XR), or mixed reality (MR) to meet branding and marketing objectives.
Description_HTML: This course was created to help students begin to put immersive media in the context of marketing communications. It helps them begin to put strategy into the framework of immersive media as platforms for advertising, events, sales, and community building. Learn to develop a framework for building immersive media marketing strategies. Specific topics include leveraging augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), extended reality (XR), or mixed reality (MR) to meet branding and marketing objectives.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Creating for Immersive Platforms
Description: In this course, students gain a comprehensive overview of the production pipeline and CG software used in the creation of virtual reality (VR) and augmented reality (AR) experiences, as well as their broader application in the metaverse. Students will explore readily available resources and develop a solid grasp of the terminology and processes involved in creating content for immersive environments.
Description_HTML: In this course, students gain a comprehensive overview of the production pipeline and CG software used in the creation of virtual reality (VR) and augmented reality (AR) experiences, as well as their broader application in the metaverse. Students will explore readily available resources and develop a solid grasp of the terminology and processes involved in creating content for immersive environments.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Advanced Curiosity for Strategists
Description: Drawing from a variety of fields, including art, anthropology, psychology, and marketing, and utilizing various techniques such as mind-mapping, this course will explore the building of intellectual curiosity as a problem-solving technique within the context of advertising and brand responsibility.
Description_HTML: Drawing from a variety of fields, including art, anthropology, psychology, and marketing, and utilizing various techniques such as mind-mapping, this course will explore the building of intellectual curiosity as a problem-solving technique within the context of advertising and brand responsibility.

Subject: JCOM
Title: Design, Technology, and Culture
Description: Through a variety of guest speakers, workshops, and modern design processes, students will examine different perspectives on how technology and advertising has evolved, why it has evolved, how it affects us personally, how these shifts affect us globally, how innovation affects policy, and how these all shape our communities.
Description_HTML: Through a variety of guest speakers, workshops, and modern design processes, students will examine different perspectives on how technology and advertising has evolved, why it has evolved, how it affects us personally, how these shifts affect us globally, how innovation affects policy, and how these all shape our communities.

Subject: JPN
Title: Workplace Communication in Japan I
Description: Students will acquire essential linguistic skills, social etiquette, and practical knowledge to effectively perform basic business tasks in a Japanese context. This course covers fundamental aspects of Japanese business culture and its perception, alongside an exploration of cultural differences and potential communication and business challenges with Japanese counterparts. Key topics include first-time meetings, building business relationships, and understanding workplace hierarchies. JPN 260 and JPN 261 don't replace JPN 201 /202/203, and won't count towards the BA 2nd Language requirement. JPN 260 and JPN 261 do not need to be taken in order.
Additional_Information: Cultural Literacy: Global Perspectives

Subject: JPN
Title: Workplace Communication in Japan II
Description: Students will acquire essential linguistic skills, social etiquette, and practical knowledge to effectively perform basic business tasks in a Japanese context. This course covers fundamental aspects of Japanese business culture and its perception, alongside an exploration of cultural differences and potential communication and business challenges with Japanese counterparts. Key topics include entertaining business partners, promoting inclusiveness in the workplace, understanding company benefits, and effective workplace communication. JPN 260 and JPN 261 don't replace JPN 201 /202/203, and won't count towards the BA 2nd Language requirement. JPN 260 and JPN 261 do not have to be taken in order.

Subject: LA
Title: Introduction to Landscape Architecture
Description: Exploring the background and scope of the profession: its history, ethics, goals, skills, topics, achievements, and evolving challenges in making healthy, functional, and beautiful places.
Description_HTML: Exploring the background and scope of the profession: its history, ethics, goals, skills, topics, achievements, and evolving challenges in making healthy, functional, and beautiful places.

Subject: LA
Title: Understanding Landscapes
Description: The goal of this course is to expand your understanding, perception, and knowledge of landscapes as cultural artifacts and the physical form of invisible processes, histories, culture, people, animals, politics, and events.
Additional_Information: Arts & Letters Area
Description_HTML:
The goal of this course is to expand your understanding, perception, and knowledge of landscapes as cultural artifacts and the physical form of invisible processes, histories, culture, people, animals, politics, and events.

 Arts & Letters Area

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Architectural Design
Description: Study of places, their use, and how they evolve. Fundamentals of environmental awareness, social factors, and small-scale site design; abstract design and elementary graphic techniques.
Description_HTML:
Study of places, their use, and how they evolve. Fundamentals of environmental awareness, social factors, and small-scale site design; abstract design and elementary graphic techniques.
Repeatable 99 times

Subject: LA
Title: Plants: Fall
Description: Characteristics, identification, and design uses of deciduous trees, shrubs, vines, and ground covers. Emphasis on identification and appropriate use in landscape design.
Description_HTML: Characteristics, identification, and design uses of deciduous trees, shrubs, vines, and ground covers. Emphasis on identification and appropriate use in landscape design.

Subject: LA
Title: Winter Plants
Description: The course will cover characteristics, identification, and design uses of ornamental conifers and broad-leaved evergreen trees, shrubs, and ground covers.
Description_HTML: The course will cover characteristics, identification, and design uses of ornamental conifers and broad-leaved evergreen trees, shrubs, and ground covers.

Subject: LA
Title: Spring Plants
Description: Focuses on flowering plants, their identification, design use, and ecosystem services they provide. Plant identification focuses on flowering trees and shrubs, groundcovers and perennials, with the intention of understanding how flowering plants may be used in design to support both human needs and ecosystem functions.
Description_HTML: Focuses on flowering plants, their identification, design use, and ecosystem services they provide. Plant identification focuses on flowering trees and shrubs, groundcovers and perennials, with the intention of understanding how flowering plants may be used in design to support both human needs and ecosystem functions.

Title: Landscape Field Work: [Topic]

Repeatable: Repeatable 2 times for a maximum of 12 credits
Description_HTML:
Direct examination and appraisal of the function, form, content, and composition of example landscapes in relation to ecological, cultural, legal, technical, aesthetic, and economic objectives.

Title: Landscape Media

Title: Digital Landscape Media

Title: Contemporary American Landscape

Description_HTML: Exploring the impact and subsequent reversal of industrialized food systems through community driven production, distribution and equity methods, foodshed resiliency creation and ecologically literate agriculture practice.

Title: Landscape Architectural Design and Process

Repeatable: Repeatable 4 times for a maximum of 30 credits
Description_HTML:
Intermediate problems in landscape architecture design. Relations among problem concepts, goals, design theory, communication media, and technical analysis.

Repeatable 4 times for a maximum of 30 credits

Subject: LA
Title: Introduction to Landscape Planning Analysis
Description: Principles of designing land- and waterscapes for human use and settlement. Ecological, social, and economic analyses of landscapes, resources, and patterns of occupancy in the Eugene-Springfield area.
Description_HTML:
Principles of designing land- and waterscapes for human use and settlement. Ecological, social, and economic analyses of landscapes, resources, and patterns of occupancy in the Eugene-Springfield area.
Requisites: Prereq: LA 413.

Subject: LA
Title: Principles of Applied Ecology
Description: Application of ecological concepts to landscape design, planning, and management. Emphasis on spatially explicit problem-solving over a range of spatial and temporal scales.
Description_HTML:
Application of ecological concepts to landscape design, planning, and management. Emphasis on spatially explicit problem-solving over a range of spatial and temporal scales.
Requisites: Prereq: one course in ecology.

Subject: LA
Title: Advanced Landscape Media: [Topic]
Description: Advanced landscape media skills in a variety of media, including 2D and 3D digital skills, visual data representation, and remote data sensing.
Repeatable: Repeatable 3 times for a maximum of 12 credits when topic changes
Description_HTML:
Advanced landscape media skills in a variety of media, including 2D and 3D digital skills, visual data representation, and remote data sensing.

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Media I
Description: Landscape Media I is the first course in a yearlong foundational landscape media sequence. The course focuses on fundamental concepts of cartography, diagramming movement and change, and conceptually representing design ideas, and introduces students to graphic design fundamentals. Students cannot receive credit for both LA 451 and ENVD 451.
Description_HTML:
Landscape Media I is the first course in a yearlong foundational landscape media sequence. The course focuses on fundamental concepts of cartography, diagramming movement and change, and conceptually representing design ideas, and introduces students to graphic design fundamentals. Students cannot receive credit for both LA 451 and ENVD 451.
Equivalent to: ENVD 451

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Media II
Description: Landscape Media II is the second course in a yearlong foundational landscape media sequence. The course explores a variety of media, computer applications, analog approaches, workflows, and theoretical ideas in the context of landform, topography, and grading as both a design process and representation product.
Description_HTML: Landscape Media II is the second course in a yearlong foundational landscape media sequence. The course explores a variety of media, computer applications, analog approaches, workflows, and theoretical ideas in the context of landform, topography, and grading as both a design process and representation product.

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Media III
Description: Landscape Media III is the third course in a yearlong foundational landscape media sequence. The course focuses on the visual communication of constructing the landscape, from sketching materials and assemblies, to to preparing construction documentation drawings.
Description_HTML: Landscape Media III is the third course in a yearlong foundational landscape media sequence. The course focuses on the visual communication of constructing the landscape, from sketching materials and assemblies, to to preparing construction documentation drawings.

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Technology Topics
Description: Intensive study of topics in landscape construction and maintenance. Topics include irrigation, lighting, special structures, water management, and road design.
Repeatable: Repeatable 3 times for a maximum of 10 credits
Description_HTML:
Intensive study of topics in landscape construction and maintenance. Topics include irrigation, lighting, special structures, water management, and road design.
Repeatable 3 times for a maximum of 10 credits

Subject: LA
Title: Professional Practice Landscape Architecture
Description: Introduces students to key aspects of professional practice, fundamental professional skills, and professional career planning. Includes licensure, legal aspects of landscape architecture, career options, business management, and project management.
Description_HTML: Introduces students to key aspects of professional practice, fundamental professional skills, and professional career planning. Includes licensure, legal aspects of landscape architecture, career options, business management, and project management.

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Materials and Construction I
Description: Landscape Materials and Construction I is the first course in a three-term sequence, with a primary focus on plants as a medium of landscape architectural design. It is an introduction to plants from the point of view of the landscape architect or architectural designer.
Description_HTML: Landscape Materials and Construction I is the first course in a three-term sequence, with a primary focus on plants as a medium of landscape architectural design. It is an introduction to plants from the point of view of the landscape architect or architectural designer.

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Materials and Construction II
Description: Landscape Materials and Construction I is the second of the three-term long Landscape Materials and Construction sequence, with a primary focus on landform as a medium of landscape architectural design and landscape engineering for accessibility, safety, and sustainability.
Description_HTML:
Landscape Materials and Construction I is the second of the three-term long Landscape Materials and Construction sequence, with a primary focus on landform as a medium of landscape architectural design and landscape engineering for accessibility, safety, and sustainability.
Requisites: Prereq: LA 464.

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Materials and Construction III
Description: Landscape Materials and Construction III is the third of the three-term long Landscape Materials and Construction sequence, with a primary focus on structures, material assemblies, and vegetation on and in buildings as media of landscape architectural design.
Description_HTML:
Landscape Materials and Construction III is the third of the three-term long Landscape Materials and Construction sequence, with a primary focus on structures, material assemblies, and vegetation on and in buildings as media of landscape architectural design.

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Architectural Theory: [Topic]
Description: This course examines theories and the role of theory in landscape architecture. Each offering explores landscape theory through a different lens, including the analysis and design of landscapes, the creation of built works, and the discussion and critique of projects.
Repeatable: Repeatable 3 times for a maximum of 16 credits
Description_HTML:
This course examines theories and the role of theory in landscape architecture. Each offering explores landscape theory through a different lens, including the analysis and design of landscapes, the creation of built works, and the discussion and critique of projects.

Subject: LA
Title: History of Landscape Architecture I
Description: First in a sequence covering the history of landscape architecture. Explores the history and theory of the designed landscape from the beginnings of human settlement to the 19th century.
Description_HTML: First in a sequence covering the history of landscape architecture. Explores the history and theory of the designed landscape from the beginnings of human settlement to the 19th century.

Subject: LA
Title: History of Landscape Architecture II
Description: The second in a sequence of two classes covering the history of landscape architecture, from the 19th century to the late 20th century.
Description_HTML:
The second in a sequence of two classes covering the history of landscape architecture, from the 19th century to the late 20th century.

Subject: LA
Title: Cultural Landscapes
Description: This course examines cultural landscapes: how we define them, how we develop a deeper understanding of them, how they are recognized and incorporated into the historic preservation and design fields, and methodologies for treating and managing their dynamic character. Understanding cultural landscapes bridges many disciplines including history, geography, ecology, landscape architecture, architecture, archaeology, and ethnography.
Description_HTML: This course examines cultural landscapes: how we define them, how we develop a deeper understanding of them, how they are recognized and incorporated into the historic preservation and design fields, and methodologies for treating and managing their dynamic character. Understanding cultural landscapes bridges many disciplines including history, geography, ecology, landscape architecture, architecture, archaeology, and ethnography.

Title: Site Planning and Design
Description: Advanced problems in landscape architecture, cultural determinants of site planning and design, design development and natural systems and processes as indicators of carrying capacity.

Description_HTML: Landscape Media III is the third course in a yearlong foundational landscape media sequence. The course focuses on the visual communication of constructing the landscape, from sketching materials and assemblies, to to preparing construction documentation drawings.

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Technology Topics
Description: Intensive study of topics in landscape construction and maintenance. Topics include irrigation, lighting, special structures, water management, and road design.
Repeatable: Repeatable 3 times for a maximum of 10 credits
Description_HTML:
Intensive study of topics in landscape construction and maintenance. Topics include irrigation, lighting, special structures, water management, and road design.
Repeatable 3 times for a maximum of 10 credits

Subject: LA
Title: Professional Practice of Landscape Archtecture
Description: Introduces students to key aspects of professional practice, fundamental professional skills, and professional career planning. Includes licensure, legal aspects of landscape architecture, career options, business management, and project management.
Description_HTML: Introduces students to key aspects of professional practice, fundamental professional skills, and professional career planning. Includes licensure, legal aspects of landscape architecture, career options, business management, and project management.

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Materials and Construction I
Description: Landscape Materials and Construction I is the first course in a three-term sequence, with a primary focus on plants as a medium of landscape architectural design. It is an introduction to plants from the point of view of the landscape architect or architectural designer.
Description_HTML: Landscape Materials and Construction I is the first course in a three-term sequence, with a primary focus on plants as a medium of landscape architectural design. It is an introduction to plants from the point of view of the landscape architect or architectural designer.

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Materials and Construction II
Description: Landscape Materials and Construction I is the second of the three-term long Landscape Materials and Construction sequence, with a primary focus on landform as a medium of landscape architectural design and landscape engineering for accessibility, safety, and sustainability.
Description_HTML: Landscape Materials and Construction I is the second of the three-term long Landscape Materials and Construction sequence, with a primary focus on landform as a medium of landscape architectural design and landscape engineering for accessibility, safety, and sustainability.

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Materials and Construction III
Description: Landscape Materials and Construction III is the third of the three-term long Landscape Materials and Construction sequence, with a primary focus on structures, material assemblies, and vegetation on and in buildings as media of landscape architectural design.
Description_HTML: Landscape Materials and Construction III is the third of the three-term long Landscape Materials and Construction sequence, with a primary focus on structures, material assemblies, and vegetation on and in buildings as media of landscape architectural design.

Subject: LA
Title: Landscape Architectural Theory: [Topic]
Description: This course examines theories and the role of theory in landscape architecture. Each offering explores landscape theory through a different lens, including the analysis and design of landscapes, the creation of built works, and the discussion and critique of projects.
Repeatable: Repeatable 3 times for a maximum of 16 credits
Description_HTML:
This course examines theories and the role of theory in landscape architecture. Each offering explores landscape theory through a different lens, including the analysis and design of landscapes, the creation of built works, and the discussion and critique of projects.
Repeatable 3 times for a maximum of 16 credits

Subject: LA
Title: History of Landscape Architecture I
Description: First in a sequence covering the history of landscape architecture. Explores the history and theory of the designed landscape from the beginnings of human settlement to the 19th century.
Description_HTML: First in a sequence covering the history of landscape architecture. Explores the history and theory of the designed landscape from the beginnings of human settlement to the 19th century.

Subject: LA
Title: History of Landscape Architecture II
Description: The second in a sequence of two classes covering the history of landscape architecture, from the 19th century to the late 20th century.
Description_HTML: The second in a sequence of two classes covering the history of landscape architecture, from the 19th century to the late 20th century.

Subject: LA
Title: Cultural Landscapes
Description: This course examines cultural landscapes: how we define them, how we develop a deeper understanding of them, how they are recognized and incorporated into the historic preservation and design fields, and methodologies for treating and managing their dynamic character. Understanding cultural landscapes bridges many disciplines including history, geography, ecology, landscape architecture, architecture, archaeology, and ethnography.
Description_HTML: This course examines cultural landscapes: how we define them, how we develop a deeper understanding of them, how they are recognized and incorporated into the historic preservation and design fields, and methodologies for treating and managing their dynamic character. Understanding cultural landscapes bridges many disciplines including history, geography, ecology, landscape architecture, architecture, archaeology, and ethnography.

Subject: LA
Title: Site Planning and Design
Description: Advanced problems in landscape architecture, cultural determinants of site planning and design, design development and natural systems and processes as indicators of carrying capacity.
Description_HTML:

Subject: LAW
Title: Human Rights, Law, and Culture
Description: The history, theory, and practice of human rights from a global perspective.

Subject: LAW
Title: Law, Justice and Accountability After Conflict
Description: Historical and theoretical overview of the conflicts and international mechanisms, with a focus on cultural, historical, and legal forces that shape postconflict peace-building efforts.
Description_HTML: Historical and theoretical overview of the conflicts and international mechanisms, with a focus on cultural, historical, and legal forces that shape postconflict peace-building efforts.

Subject: LAW
Title: The Death Penalty
Description: Explores the use of capital punishment in the United States, focusing on the law governing the death penalty, in the context of the criminal justice system, social science and policy.
Description_HTML: Explores the use of capital punishment in the United States, focusing on the law governing the death penalty, in the context of the criminal justice system, social science and policy.

Subject: LAW
Title: Race and the Courts
Description: This course examines intersections of race and the law. Students will explore historical and contemporary contexts to understand how institutional frameworks can promote and hinder social reform.
Description_HTML: This course examines intersections of race and the law. Students will explore historical and contemporary contexts to understand how institutional frameworks can promote and hinder social reform.

Subject: LAW
Title: Legal Secrets
Description: This course examines certain types of information – typically produced and owned by institutions – which are given elevated protection under the law as “secret.” Legal secrets examined in this course are distinct from notions of individual privacy, which refers to an individual’s “right to be let alone,” to protect their personal information from government intrusion, and to be free from unreasonable search and seizure.
