Subject: LAW
Title: Contracts
Description: Examines contractual relationships from formation through interpretation and breach to remedies and potential third-party rights. Covers the common law of contracts and Uniform Commercial Code, Article 2, which governs contracts for the sale of goods.
Description_HTML: Examines contractual relationships from formation through interpretation and breach to remedies and potential third-party rights. Covers the common law of contracts and Uniform Commercial Code, Article 2, which governs contracts for the sale of goods.

Subject: LAW
Title: Torts
Description: Liability for intentional and negligently caused injuries to person and property, strict liability, vicarious liability, abnormally dangerous activities, products liability, nuisance, invasion of privacy, defamation, defenses and immunities, the impact of insurance and risk distribution upon liability, accident compensation plans, damages, losses.
Description_HTML: Liability for intentional and negligently caused injuries to person and property, strict liability, vicarious liability, abnormally dangerous activities, products liability, nuisance, invasion of privacy, defamation, defenses and immunities, the impact of insurance and risk distribution upon liability, accident compensation plans, damages, losses.

Subject: LAW
Title: Advanced Torts
Description: Students study and discuss the economic and dignitary torts (for example, defamation, misrepresentation, invasion of privacy, malpractice); they are able to analyze and apply this knowledge in a classroom environment.
Description_HTML:
Students study and discuss the economic and dignitary torts (for example, defamation, misrepresentation, invasion of privacy, malpractice); they are able to analyze and apply this knowledge in a classroom environment.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 613.

Subject: LAW
Title: Civil Procedure
Description: Survey of federal court organization and jurisdiction and of systems of civil procedure.
Description_HTML: Survey of federal court organization and jurisdiction and of systems of civil procedure.

Subject: LAW
Title: Property
Description: Nature and function of private property rights. Topics may include the common law classification of estates in land; forms of concurrent ownership; landlord and tenant; adverse possession; incorporeal interests in land, easements, covenants, and servitudes; title; introduction to land use issues and judicial legislative developments in law.
Description_HTML: Nature and function of private property rights. Topics may include the common law classification of estates in land; forms of concurrent ownership; landlord and tenant; adverse possession; incorporeal interests in land, easements, covenants, and servitudes; title; introduction to land use issues and judicial legislative developments in law.

Subject: LAW
Title: Criminal Law
Description: Administration of criminal law and the definition of crimes as a technique of social order with primary basic elements of criminal liability. Emphasis on sources of definitions, limitations of culpability, and defenses.
Description_HTML: Administration of criminal law and the definition of crimes as a technique of social order with primary basic elements of criminal liability. Emphasis on sources of definitions, limitations of culpability, and defenses.

Subject: LAW
Title: White-Collar Crime
Description: For students interested in the practice of criminal law. Assists business lawyers who advise clients on the business practices that constitute criminal activity.
Description_HTML: For students interested in the practice of criminal law. Assists business lawyers who advise clients on the business practices that constitute criminal activity.

Subject: LAW
Title: Business Associations
Description: This is the introductory course on business law and a prerequisite for most upper-level business law courses. It introduces the governance structure of agency, partnerships, corporations and LLCs.
Description_HTML: This is the introductory course on business law and a prerequisite for most upper-level business law courses. It introduces the governance structure of agency, partnerships, corporations and LLCs.

Subject: LAW
Title: Legal Research and Writing I
Description: Integrated instruction in legal research, analysis, and writing of legal memoranda emphasizes research strategies, problem solving, and the relationship between research strategies and analysis. Writing assignments, each progressively more difficult, are evaluated by faculty members. Offered in small sections. Includes library workshops and individual conferences.
Description_HTML: Integrated instruction in legal research, analysis, and writing of legal memoranda emphasizes research strategies, problem solving, and the relationship between research strategies and analysis. Writing assignments, each progressively more difficult, are evaluated by faculty members. Offered in small sections. Includes library workshops and individual conferences.

Subject: LAW
Title: Legal Research and Writing II
Description: Building on the research, writing, and analytical skills of Legal Research and Writing I, students focus on persuasive writing as they produce trial memoranda and appellate briefs. Students present final oral arguments in a courtroom setting before a panel of three judges.
Description_HTML: Building on the research, writing, and analytical skills of Legal Research and Writing I, students focus on persuasive writing as they produce trial memoranda and appellate briefs. Students present final oral arguments in a courtroom setting before a panel of three judges.

Subject: LAW
Title: Advanced Legal Research
Description: Development of skills in formulating efficient research strategies online and in print; exposure to research methods in particular areas of law.
Description_HTML: Development of skills in formulating efficient research strategies online and in print; exposure to research methods in particular areas of law.

Subject: LAW
Title: Business Bankruptcy
Description: Explores the law governing business bankruptcy; examines tools for restructuring and rehabilitating a business under Chapter 11 of the Bankruptcy Code. Topics include operating a business in bankruptcy, reshaping the estate, and negotiating and confirming a plan of reorganization.
Description_HTML:
Explores the law governing business bankruptcy; examines tools for restructuring and rehabilitating a business under Chapter 11 of the Bankruptcy Code. Topics include operating a business in bankruptcy, reshaping the estate, and negotiating and confirming a plan of reorganization.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 648.

Subject: LAW
Title: Mergers and Acquisitions
Description: Participants develop an understanding of how to analyze a potential acquiree and how that analysis informs and governs the drafting of the acquisition agreement and the disclosure document. Presents the germination of a transaction from financial need to executed documentation.
Description_HTML:
Participants develop an understanding of how to analyze a potential acquiree and how that analysis informs and governs the drafting of the acquisition agreement and the disclosure document. Presents the germination of a transaction from financial need to executed documentation.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 620.

Subject: LAW
Title: Accounting for Lawyers
Description: Covers the accounting cycle, generally accepted accounting principles, financial statements, and common legal and accounting topics.
Description_HTML: Covers the accounting cycle, generally accepted accounting principles, financial statements, and common legal and accounting topics.

Subject: LAW
Title: Nonprofit Organizations
Description: Provides an overview of the theory, purposes, and regulation of nonprofit organizations. Practical skills are developed through drafting assignments.
Description_HTML: Provides an overview of the theory, purposes, and regulation of nonprofit organizations. Practical skills are developed through drafting assignments.

Subject: LAW
Title: Fundamentals of Loans
Description: Covers the rights of borrowers and lenders in unsecured and secured loans in less depth than the Secured Transactions course.
Description_HTML: Covers the rights of borrowers and lenders in unsecured and secured loans in less depth than the Secured Transactions course.

Subject: LAW
Title: Tax Policy
Description: Explores tax policy issues including how taxes are used to create social equality. Evaluates the current tax system using the three classic goals: equity, efficiency, and simplification.
Description_HTML: Explores tax policy issues including how taxes are used to create social equality. Evaluates the current tax system using the three classic goals: equity, efficiency, and simplification.

Subject: LAW
Title: Sales
Description: Covers contracts for the sale of goods as codified in Uniform Commercial Code Article 2; international contracts, regulation of consumer warranties, leases of goods under Article 2A.
Description_HTML: Covers contracts for the sale of goods as codified in Uniform Commercial Code Article 2; international contracts, regulation of consumer warranties, leases of goods under Article 2A.

Subject: LAW
Title: Business Planning
Description: Study of business life cycle from its initial organization and operation to its eventual sale and dissolution. Students draft documents for use in hypothetical transactions, compare the way partnerships and corporations deal with similar problems, and analyze the impact taxes have on business decisions.
Description_HTML:
Study of business life cycle from its initial organization and operation to its eventual sale and dissolution. Students draft documents for use in hypothetical transactions, compare the way partnerships and corporations deal with similar problems, and analyze the impact taxes have on business decisions.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 620, LAW 680.

Subject: LAW
Title: Contract Drafting
Description: The principles of contemporary commercial drafting, introduction to documents typically used in a variety of transactions.
Description_HTML: The principles of contemporary commercial drafting, introduction to documents typically used in a variety of transactions.

Subject: LAW
Title: Secured Transactions
Description: Examines the rules that govern borrowing and lending with collateral; buying and selling promissory notes and other debt; and related transactions that are essential to large and small transactions.
Description_HTML: Examines the rules that govern borrowing and lending with collateral; buying and selling promissory notes and other debt; and related transactions that are essential to large and small transactions.

Subject: LAW
Title: Trusts and Estates I
Description: Basics of estate planning law--intestate succession, wills, will substitutes, and trusts.
Description_HTML:
Basics of estate planning law--intestate succession, wills, will substitutes, and trusts.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 617.

Subject: LAW
Title: Workers' Compensation Law
Description: Fundamentals of workers’ compensation law in Oregon and other states.
Description_HTML: Fundamentals of workers’ compensation law in Oregon and other states.

Subject: LAW
Title: Legislation and Statutory Interpretation
Description: Covers theories of the legislative process, normative theories of statutory interpretation, and the main judicial doctrines of statutory interpretation.
Description_HTML: Covers theories of the legislative process, normative theories of statutory interpretation, and the main judicial doctrines of statutory interpretation.

Subject: LAW
Title: International Business Transactions
Description: Examines legal issues affecting international business activity; includes forms of doing business, trading of goods, commercial terms, the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods, financing, technology, transfers, foreign investment, and dispute resolution.
Description_HTML: Examines legal issues affecting international business activity; includes forms of doing business, trading of goods, commercial terms, the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods, financing, technology, transfers, foreign investment, and dispute resolution.

Subject: LAW
Title: Constitutional Law I
Description: Government structure and individual rights are examined in the context of the authority of courts to declare legislative acts unconstitutional. Includes congressional regulatory power under the Commerce Clause, implied limits on state regulatory power, and the substantive dimensions of due process.
Description_HTML: Government structure and individual rights are examined in the context of the authority of courts to declare legislative acts unconstitutional. Includes congressional regulatory power under the Commerce Clause, implied limits on state regulatory power, and the substantive dimensions of due process.

Subject: LAW
Title: Constitutional Law II
Description: Guarantees of individual rights against government, especially freedom of expression and equal protection of the law.
Description_HTML:
Guarantees of individual rights against government, especially freedom of expression and equal protection of the law.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 643.

Subject: LAW
Title: Oregon Practice and Procedure
Description: Intensive study of civil procedure in Oregon courts, and the critical evaluation of Oregon civil procedure in light of the purposes and values of a procedural system and in comparison with federal rules.
Description_HTML: Intensive study of civil procedure in Oregon courts, and the critical evaluation of Oregon civil procedure in light of the purposes and values of a procedural system and in comparison with federal rules.

Subject: LAW
Title: Federal Jurisdiction
Description: Addresses the role of federal courts in the operation of the federal system. Includes analysis of constitutional and legislative foundations of the judicial power of the United States; jurisdiction--diversity of citizenship, federal question, jurisdictional amount, and removal; venue; federal and state court relationships; the law applied to federal courts; procedure in the federal district courts; appellate jurisdiction and procedure in courts of appeals and the Supreme Court.
Description_HTML: Addresses the role of federal courts in the operation of the federal system. Includes analysis of constitutional and legislative foundations of the judicial power of the United States; jurisdiction--diversity of citizenship, federal question, jurisdictional amount, and removal; venue; federal and state court relationships; the law applied to federal courts; procedure in the federal district courts; appellate jurisdiction and procedure in courts of appeals and the Supreme Court.

Subject: LAW
Title: Bankruptcy
Description: Introduction to bankruptcy law; focuses on consumer bankruptcy and contrasting creditor's rights and debtor's protections under the federal Bankruptcy Code with those under state collection law. Recommended preparation: Commercial Law ( LAW 636 ).
Description_HTML: Introduction to bankruptcy law; focuses on consumer bankruptcy and contrasting creditor's rights and debtor's protections under the federal Bankruptcy Code with those under state collection law. Recommended preparation: Commercial Law (LAW 636).

Subject: LAW
Title: Legal Profession
Description: Addresses the Model Code of Professional Responsibility, the Code of Judicial Ethics, roles and functions of lawyers in society, organization and functions of the bar, provision of legal service, responsibilities in representing clients, and the future of the legal profession. It may include the review and analysis of videotaped ethical problems.
Description_HTML: Addresses the Model Code of Professional Responsibility, the Code of Judicial Ethics, roles and functions of lawyers in society, organization and functions of the bar, provision of legal service, responsibilities in representing clients, and the future of the legal profession. It may include the review and analysis of videotaped ethical problems.

Subject: LAW
Title: Trial Practice
Description: Introduces the essential techniques and theory necessary to conduct a trial in court.
Description_HTML:
Introduces the essential techniques and theory necessary to conduct a trial in court.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 652.

Subject: LAW
Title: Evidence
Description: Covers the structure of the adversary system; roles of judge, jury, and attorney in the fact-finding process; sufficiency of evidence; order of proof; presumptions; relevancy; judicial notice; real and documentary evidence; form and elicitation of oral testimony; impeachment and rehabilitation of witnesses; the hearsay rule and its exceptions; privileges. Addresses practical problems in the introduction of evidence and trial tactics and methods. Courtroom observations, movies, and videotapes of effective trial techniques present realistic situations.
Description_HTML: Covers the structure of the adversary system; roles of judge, jury, and attorney in the fact-finding process; sufficiency of evidence; order of proof; presumptions; relevancy; judicial notice; real and documentary evidence; form and elicitation of oral testimony; impeachment and rehabilitation of witnesses; the hearsay rule and its exceptions; privileges. Addresses practical problems in the introduction of evidence and trial tactics and methods. Courtroom observations, movies, and videotapes of effective trial techniques present realistic situations.

Subject: LAW
Title: Climate Change Law and Policy
Description: This course examines laws and policies in the United States over the last half century related to climate change, including international agreements that the nation has joined or considered joining.
Description_HTML: This course examines laws and policies in the United States over the last half century related to climate change, including international agreements that the nation has joined or considered joining.

Subject: LAW
Title: Family Law
Description: Marriage and its legal consequences, divorce and its financial consequences; establishing the parent-child relationship; child custody and child support; jurisdiction and choice of law issues at divorce; legal regulation of marriage; rights of unmarried cohabitants.
Description_HTML: Marriage and its legal consequences, divorce and its financial consequences; establishing the parent-child relationship; child custody and child support; jurisdiction and choice of law issues at divorce; legal regulation of marriage; rights of unmarried cohabitants.

Subject: LAW
Title: Elder Law
Description: Topics include social security and pensions; health care decision-making, including the right to die, living wills, and durable powers of attorney for health care; planning for health care financing, including alternate living arrangements and financing through private resources, Medicare and Medicaid; regulation of retirement facilities and nursing homes; and protection of disabled adults through guardianships, conservator-ships, and related mechanisms. Covers the basic and comprehensive writing requirements.
Description_HTML: Topics include social security and pensions; health care decision-making, including the right to die, living wills, and durable powers of attorney for health care; planning for health care financing, including alternate living arrangements and financing through private resources, Medicare and Medicaid; regulation of retirement facilities and nursing homes; and protection of disabled adults through guardianships, conservator-ships, and related mechanisms. Covers the basic and comprehensive writing requirements.

Subject: LAW
Title: Local Government Law
Description: Uses DeTocqueville and Madison to frame the issue of decentralization versus centralization in governmental structure. Course materials are divided into three sections: 1) vertical governmental organization--the relationships between cities and state governments and the relation of both to the federal government; 2) horizontal governmental relations--how neighboring cities deal with one another on public school funding, exclusionary zoning, regional planning, and other areas; 3) internal relationship between cities and their citizens--voting systems, when citizens can sue a city, referenda, and initiatives.
Description_HTML: Uses DeTocqueville and Madison to frame the issue of decentralization versus centralization in governmental structure. Course materials are divided into three sections: 1) vertical governmental organization--the relationships between cities and state governments and the relation of both to the federal government; 2) horizontal governmental relations--how neighboring cities deal with one another on public school funding, exclusionary zoning, regional planning, and other areas; 3) internal relationship between cities and their citizens--voting systems, when citizens can sue a city, referenda, and initiatives.

Subject: LAW
Title: Labor Law
Description: Analysis of the National Labor Relations Act and the Oregon Labor Relations Act; the right of self-organization; selection of the representative by election and by other means; unit determination; bargaining in good faith; remedies for unfair labor practices; judicial review; strikes, boycotts, and lockouts under various labor relations acts; concerted activities; and roles of courts and labor agencies.
Description_HTML: Analysis of the National Labor Relations Act and the Oregon Labor Relations Act; the right of self-organization; selection of the representative by election and by other means; unit determination; bargaining in good faith; remedies for unfair labor practices; judicial review; strikes, boycotts, and lockouts under various labor relations acts; concerted activities; and roles of courts and labor agencies.

Subject: LAW
Title: Employment Law
Description: Examines individual rights in the workplace, including federal and state statutes. Use of questionnaires, polygraph legislation, drug and other medical tests; employment discrimination (Title 7); disability discrimination; family leave statutes; and a variety of working conditions are covered, including harassment, workplace privacy, and free speech as well as the Occupational Safety and Health Act. The doctrine of at-will discharge and whistle-blower legislation are included.
Description_HTML: Examines individual rights in the workplace, including federal and state statutes. Use of questionnaires, polygraph legislation, drug and other medical tests; employment discrimination (Title 7); disability discrimination; family leave statutes; and a variety of working conditions are covered, including harassment, workplace privacy, and free speech as well as the Occupational Safety and Health Act. The doctrine of at-will discharge and whistle-blower legislation are included.

Subject: LAW
Title: Remedies
Description: Remedies available for prevention of redress of civil wrongs; includes monetary damages; restitutionary remedies such as tracing, constructive trusts, equitable liens, and injunctions.
Description_HTML: Remedies available for prevention of redress of civil wrongs; includes monetary damages; restitutionary remedies such as tracing, constructive trusts, equitable liens, and injunctions.

Subject: LAW
Title: Jurisprudence
Description: Topics may include examination of important conceptual theories of law--legal positivism, natural law, legal realism; the relation of law and morality; theories of justice: economic, Kantian, utlitarian; the Critical Legal Studies movement; philosophical aspects of legal issues; abortion and punishment; feminist theories of law; and moral constraints of the practice of law.
Description_HTML: Topics may include examination of important conceptual theories of law--legal positivism, natural law, legal realism; the relation of law and morality; theories of justice: economic, Kantian, utlitarian; the Critical Legal Studies movement; philosophical aspects of legal issues; abortion and punishment; feminist theories of law; and moral constraints of the practice of law.

Subject: LAW
Title: Administrative Law
Description: Analysis of judicial review of administrative action, including presumptions, standing, ripeness, exhaustion, and questions of fact and law; the process of proof in adjudicatory hearings, including official notice, evidentiary considerations, and investigation; the process of decision in adjudicatory hearings, including separation of function, bias, and ex parte communication; procedural distinctions between rule making and adjudication.
Description_HTML: Analysis of judicial review of administrative action, including presumptions, standing, ripeness, exhaustion, and questions of fact and law; the process of proof in adjudicatory hearings, including official notice, evidentiary considerations, and investigation; the process of decision in adjudicatory hearings, including separation of function, bias, and ex parte communication; procedural distinctions between rule making and adjudication.

Subject: LAW
Title: Securities Regulation
Description: Examines the federal statutes and regulations that affect the initial and secondary distribution of securities. Emphasis is placed on the Securities Act of 1933, the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, and the integrated disclosure system now largely in place.
Description_HTML: Examines the federal statutes and regulations that affect the initial and secondary distribution of securities. Emphasis is placed on the Securities Act of 1933, the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, and the integrated disclosure system now largely in place.

Subject: LAW
Title: Copyrights
Description: Virtually any creative product (other than inventions) that originates with the author can be protected by copyright. Comprehensive federal copyright statute is built upon extensive judicial interpretations through case law. Considers creations, ownership, and transfer of copyright interests and the rights accorded to copyright owners to make copies and derivative works and to distribute, perform, and display the work. Addresses the basic principles of trademark law.
Description_HTML: Virtually any creative product (other than inventions) that originates with the author can be protected by copyright. Comprehensive federal copyright statute is built upon extensive judicial interpretations through case law. Considers creations, ownership, and transfer of copyright interests and the rights accorded to copyright owners to make copies and derivative works and to distribute, perform, and display the work. Addresses the basic principles of trademark law.

Subject: LAW
Title: Land Use Law
Description: Surveys the function, operation, and legal impact of state and local public planning and land-use control laws, ordinances, and administrative growth-control techniques; transfer of developmental rights; zoning; variances; conditional-use permits; and nonconforming uses. Considers newer state-level land-use control devices, such as state environmental impact assessment acts (e.g., in California) and statewide land-use planning laws (e.g., in Oregon).
Description_HTML: Surveys the function, operation, and legal impact of state and local public planning and land-use control laws, ordinances, and administrative growth-control techniques; transfer of developmental rights; zoning; variances; conditional-use permits; and nonconforming uses. Considers newer state-level land-use control devices, such as state environmental impact assessment acts (e.g., in California) and statewide land-use planning laws (e.g., in Oregon).

Subject: LAW
Title: Water Resources Law
Description: Reparian and appropriation water law systems, federal and state power over water resources, transfer of water rights, groundwater management, public water rights, including the public trust doctrine, and enviornmental constraints on water use.
Description_HTML: Reparian and appropriation water law systems, federal and state power over water resources, transfer of water rights, groundwater management, public water rights, including the public trust doctrine, and enviornmental constraints on water use.

Subject: LAW
Title: International Law
Description: Justification for state actions labeled rules of law; sources and evidence of a law between states; statehood; treaties; state responsibility and authority; individuals in transnational situations; international cooperation; protection of human rights; and use of military force.
Description_HTML: Justification for state actions labeled rules of law; sources and evidence of a law between states; statehood; treaties; state responsibility and authority; individuals in transnational situations; international cooperation; protection of human rights; and use of military force.

Subject: LAW
Title: Patent Law and Policy
Description: Developments in patent law including patentable subject matter; requirements for patentability and infringement; the process of obtaining and enforcing a patent; and contemporary controversies in patent law, such as ethical and economic objections to biotechnology and software patents.
Description_HTML: Developments in patent law including patentable subject matter; requirements for patentability and infringement; the process of obtaining and enforcing a patent; and contemporary controversies in patent law, such as ethical and economic objections to biotechnology and software patents.

Subject: LAW
Title: Intellectual Property Licensing
Description: Course explores business, legal, and negotiating issues in intellectual property licensing agreements. Students gain an overview of intellectual property law, pertinent areas of contract law, business factors, and evaluation issues.
Description_HTML: Course explores business, legal, and negotiating issues in intellectual property licensing agreements. Students gain an overview of intellectual property law, pertinent areas of contract law, business factors, and evaluation issues.

Subject: LAW
Title: Legal Writing
Description: Research and writing supervised by a faculty member. Typically 2 credits, but never more than 3, are awarded for a writing project in one semester.
Description_HTML:
Research and writing supervised by a faculty member. Typically 2 credits, but never more than 3, are awarded for a writing project in one semester.
Repeatable 99 times

Subject: LAW
Title: Indian Law
Description: Provides students with an understanding and overview of the fundamental principles of American Indian law.
Description_HTML: Provides students with an understanding and overview of the fundamental principles of American Indian law.

Subject: LAW
Title: Ocean and Coastal Law
Description: Surveys federal, state, and international laws governing the use and protection of ocean and coastal resources including relevant judicial decisions, administrative regulations, and management plans.
Description_HTML: Surveys federal, state, and international laws governing the use and protection of ocean and coastal resources including relevant judicial decisions, administrative regulations, and management plans.

Subject: LAW
Title: Federal Income Tax I
Description: Statutory, judicial, and administrative material related to individual income tax—concepts of income, deductions, credits, tax accounting, basis, and capital gains and losses.
Description_HTML: Statutory, judicial, and administrative material related to individual income tax—concepts of income, deductions, credits, tax accounting, basis, and capital gains and losses.

Subject: LAW
Title: Federal Income Tax II
Description: Tax treatment of partners and partnerships, corporations, and shareholders.
Description_HTML:
Tax treatment of partners and partnerships, corporations, and shareholders.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 680.

Subject: LAW
Title: Estate Planning
Description: Presents problems in estate analysis, planning, and execution; planning an estate from the interview stage to the drafting of wills and trusts to implement the estate plan.
Description_HTML:
Presents problems in estate analysis, planning, and execution; planning an estate from the interview stage to the drafting of wills and trusts to implement the estate plan.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 637.

Subject: LAW
Title: Criminal Investigation
Description: Examines the regulation of law enforcement investigatory practices-- searches and seizures, the eliciting of confessions, and lineups and other idenfication procedures. Course materials analyze various constitutional and statutory constraints on law enforcement practices, and deal extensively with landmark federal constitutional cases such as Miranda v. Arizona.
Description_HTML: Examines the regulation of law enforcement investigatory practices-- searches and seizures, the eliciting of confessions, and lineups and other idenfication procedures. Course materials analyze various constitutional and statutory constraints on law enforcement practices, and deal extensively with landmark federal constitutional cases such as Miranda v. Arizona.

Subject: LAW
Title: Criminal Adjudication
Description: Examines the adjudicative part of criminal procedure; covers the decision to charge, bail and pretrial release, grand juries and preliminary hearings, discovery, pretrial motions, plea bargaining, jury trials, appeals, and former jeopardy.
Description_HTML: Examines the adjudicative part of criminal procedure; covers the decision to charge, bail and pretrial release, grand juries and preliminary hearings, discovery, pretrial motions, plea bargaining, jury trials, appeals, and former jeopardy.

Subject: LAW
Title: International Environmental Law
Description: Investigates treaty and customary principles of international law regarding environmental protection. Covers problems of protecting the international environmental commons, transboundary pollution, and international interest in national environmental resources.
Description_HTML: Investigates treaty and customary principles of international law regarding environmental protection. Covers problems of protecting the international environmental commons, transboundary pollution, and international interest in national environmental resources.

Subject: LAW
Title: Human Rights and Environment
Description: Environmental rights, increasingly recognized as a new category of human rights as well as an application of existing rights, are both substantive and procedural. Presents recent developments in international law and national law in various countries in Europe, Africa, Asia, and the Americas. Examines international instruments, national constitutions, and legislation. Discussion includes novel international court cases that interpret and apply these rights.
Description_HTML: Environmental rights, increasingly recognized as a new category of human rights as well as an application of existing rights, are both substantive and procedural. Presents recent developments in international law and national law in various countries in Europe, Africa, Asia, and the Americas. Examines international instruments, national constitutions, and legislation. Discussion includes novel international court cases that interpret and apply these rights.

Subject: LAW
Title: Professional Sports Law
Description: Is the NFL an illegal cartel? What rights do players and unions have? Explores the regulation of professional leagues, players, teams, coaches, and agents.
Description_HTML: Is the NFL an illegal cartel? What rights do players and unions have? Explores the regulation of professional leagues, players, teams, coaches, and agents.

Subject: LAW
Title: Amateur Sports Law
Description: Are student athletes pampered or victimized? What has Title IX accomplished? What’s next for the National Collegiate Athletic Association? Explores the regulation of intercollegiate and interscholastic sports.
Description_HTML: Are student athletes pampered or victimized? What has Title IX accomplished? What’s next for the National Collegiate Athletic Association? Explores the regulation of intercollegiate and interscholastic sports.

Subject: LAW
Title: Sports Licensing
Description: A practical look into the world of sports licensing. The focus will be on examining real-world contracts and the contexts in which they were negotiated.
Description_HTML: A practical look into the world of sports licensing. The focus will be on examining real-world contracts and the contexts in which they were negotiated.

Subject: LAW
Title: Consumer Law
Description: Overview of major consumer protection laws and concepts, including false advertising, consumer privacy, identity theft, credit reporting, home purchases, credit disclosures, and loan regulations.
Description_HTML: Overview of major consumer protection laws and concepts, including false advertising, consumer privacy, identity theft, credit reporting, home purchases, credit disclosures, and loan regulations.

Subject: LAW
Title: Trademark Law
Description: Introduces trademark law, focusing on U.S. federal trademark law, and examines common law trademarks and unfair competition as well as international dimensions of trademark law.
Description_HTML: Introduces trademark law, focusing on U.S. federal trademark law, and examines common law trademarks and unfair competition as well as international dimensions of trademark law.

Subject: LAW
Title: Internship: [Topic]
Description: Hollywood Externship, Federal Judicial Internship.

Hollywood Externship, Federal Judicial Internship.
Repeatable 99 times

Subject: LAW
Title: Business Law Clinic
Description: Students represent companies who need legal assistance in forming and operating their businesses under the supervision of an attorney. Includes a weekly seminar.
Description_HTML:
Students represent companies who need legal assistance in forming and operating their businesses under the supervision of an attorney. Includes a weekly seminar.

Subject: LAW
Title: Judicial Externship [Topic]
Description: Externship at the Lane County Circuit Court. Students conduct research, write legal memoranda, draft opinions and generally participate in the daily operation of the court.
Description_HTML:
Externship at the Lane County Circuit Court. Students conduct research, write legal memoranda, draft opinions and generally participate in the daily operation of the court.
Repeatable 99 times

Subject: LAW
Title: Gender-Based Violence and the Law
Description: This class focuses on domestic abuse, sexual assault, and stalking and the diverse array of laws that addresses those topics.
Description_HTML: This class focuses on domestic abuse, sexual assault, and stalking and the diverse array of laws that addresses those topics.

Subject: LAW
Title: Advocacy Oral Argument
Description: This course provides advanced instruction on oral appellate advocacy. Oral argument offers an advocate the only opportunity to hear a court’s concerns and provide persuasive response. Over the course of one week, students will develop and present components of an oral argument. At the end of the week, students will present a final oral argument to a three-judge moot court panel.
Description_HTML:
This course provides advanced instruction on oral appellate advocacy. Oral argument offers an advocate the only opportunity to hear a court’s concerns and provide persuasive response. Over the course of one week, students will develop and present components of an oral argument. At the end of the week, students will present a final oral argument to a three-judge moot court panel.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 622, LAW 623.

Subject: LAW
Title: Civil Rights Law
Description: This course explores the evolution of civil rights laws in the United States, with a focus on race, gender, and sexuality. Students will analyze landmark cases, legislation, and policy debates, while examining contemporary issues related to civil rights litigation and its impact on society.
Description_HTML: This course explores the evolution of civil rights laws in the United States, with a focus on race, gender, and sexuality. Students will analyze landmark cases, legislation, and policy debates, while examining contemporary issues related to civil rights litigation and its impact on society.

Subject: LAW
Title: Legal Reasoning and Analysis
Description: This course is designed to allow you to practice with fundamental lawyering skills in a variety of different practice situations and on the bar exam.
Description_HTML: This course is designed to allow you to practice with fundamental lawyering skills in a variety of different practice situations and on the bar exam.

Subject: LAW
Title: Plea Bargaining
Description: This seminar explores plea bargaining and its role within criminal justice, with special focus on debates over the utility, coercive aspects, ethics, constitutionality of the practice, and possibilities of reform.
Description_HTML: This seminar explores plea bargaining and its role within criminal justice, with special focus on debates over the utility, coercive aspects, ethics, constitutionality of the practice, and possibilities of reform.

Subject: LAW
Title: Alternative Dispute Resolution Litigation Strategy
Description: Provides knowledge, tools and skills for lawyers to settle cases, help clients make an informed decision about settlement, and identify appropriate processes.
Description_HTML: Provides knowledge, tools and skills for lawyers to settle cases, help clients make an informed decision about settlement, and identify appropriate processes.

Subject: LAW
Title: Arbitration
Description: This course introduces students to the theory and practice of arbitration. Students will learn the statutory framework and caselaw for arbitration in domestic and international contexts.
Description_HTML: This course introduces students to the theory and practice of arbitration. Students will learn the statutory framework and caselaw for arbitration in domestic and international contexts.

Subject: LAW
Title: Mediation
Description: This course introduces students to the theory and practice of mediation. In an experiential format, students will learn how to manage the stages of a mediation process.
Description_HTML: This course introduces students to the theory and practice of mediation. In an experiential format, students will learn how to manage the stages of a mediation process.

Subject: LAW
Title: International Sports Law I
Description: An overview of the law and structure of the organizations that govern international sports, including FIFA, the International Olympic Committee, World Anti-Doping Association, and Court of Arbitration for Sport.
Description_HTML: An overview of the law and structure of the organizations that govern international sports, including FIFA, the International Olympic Committee, World Anti-Doping Association, and Court of Arbitration for Sport.

Subject: LAW
Title: International Sports Law II
Description: A trip to Europe during J-Term to meet with and learn from foreign sports lawyers at the International Olympic Committee, World Anti-Doping Association, Court of Arbitration for Sport, and others.
Repeatable: Repeatable 1 time for a maximum of 2 credits
Description_HTML:
A trip to Europe during J-Term to meet with and learn from foreign sports lawyers at the International Olympic Committee, World Anti-Doping Association, Court of Arbitration for Sport, and others.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 726.
Repeatable 1 time for a maximum of 2 credits

Subject: LAW
Title: Advanced Appellate Advocacy
Description: This simulation course teaches students practical skills for engaging in appellate advocacy. Students focus on structuring arguments, writing and editing appellate briefs, and doing oral argument.
Description_HTML: This simulation course teaches students practical skills for engaging in appellate advocacy. Students focus on structuring arguments, writing and editing appellate briefs, and doing oral argument.

Subject: LAW
Title: Intensive Writing
Description: Introduces students to ways in which lawyers communicate and gives students the opportunity to more extensively study the mechanics of effectively communicating legal analysis.
Description_HTML: Introduces students to ways in which lawyers communicate and gives students the opportunity to more extensively study the mechanics of effectively communicating legal analysis.

Subject: LAW
Title: Writing in Law Practice
Description: Provides students with opportunities to develop practice-oriented writing skills in a variety of contexts.
Description_HTML: Provides students with opportunities to develop practice-oriented writing skills in a variety of contexts.

Subject: LAW
Title: Intensive Legal Writing
Description: Students produce documents in a wide variety of practice settings, including office memoranda, contracts, and client letters. Students receive extensive feedback and opportunities to revise their work.
Description_HTML: Students produce documents in a wide variety of practice settings, including office memoranda, contracts, and client letters. Students receive extensive feedback and opportunities to revise their work.

Subject: LAW
Title: Advanced Uniform Commercial Code
Description: Covers property-based aspects of business transactions including secured loans, set-off, and ownership and transfer of investments, from advanced U.S., international, and comparative standpoints.
Requisites: Prereq: Secured Transactions or Fundamentals of Loans or equivalent.
Description_HTML:
Covers property-based aspects of business transactions including secured loans, set-off, and ownership and transfer of investments, from advanced U.S., international, and comparative standpoints.
Requisites: Prereq: Secured Transactions or Fundamentals of Loans or equivalent.

Subject: LAW
Title: Start-Up Businesses
Description: This course familiarizes students with common issues arising in day-to-day representation of startup businesses, ranging from entity choice, commercial issues, key policies to financings, exits and crisis management.
Description_HTML:
This course familiarizes students with common issues arising in day-to-day representation of startup businesses, ranging from entity choice, commercial issues, key policies to financings, exits and crisis management.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 620.

Subject: LAW
Title: Sustainable Business
Description: This course reviews the impact of laws and social norms on the sustainability of businesses. Students will learn how business leaders, investors, legislators, regulators and other stakeholders can use legal mechanisms—regulations, fines or fees, tax incentives, tax penalties, and corporate governance rules and structures, as well as social norms—to encourage businesses to operate more sustainably.
Description_HTML: This course reviews the impact of laws and social norms on the sustainability of businesses. Students will learn how business leaders, investors, legislators, regulators and other stakeholders can use legal mechanisms—regulations, fines or fees, tax incentives, tax penalties, and corporate governance rules and structures, as well as social norms—to encourage businesses to operate more sustainably.

Subject: LAW
Title: Writing Colloquium
Description: Students give and receive feedback on writing in a workshop setting with participants discussing each others’ work. Student will explore theme, structure, and style as they learn to critique writing.
Description_HTML: Students give and receive feedback on writing in a workshop setting with participants discussing each others’ work. Student will explore theme, structure, and style as they learn to critique writing.

Subject: LAW
Title: Innovations in Criminal Justice
Description: Focuses on advanced approaches to the reduction of recidivism in the federal criminal justice system. Discussion centers on the use of therapeutic jurisprudence grounded in evidence-based practice.
Description_HTML: Focuses on advanced approaches to the reduction of recidivism in the federal criminal justice system. Discussion centers on the use of therapeutic jurisprudence grounded in evidence-based practice.

Subject: LAW
Title: Leadership Practices for Professional Success
Description: Examines leadership theories and models. Through intensive readings, exercises, introspection, and open discussion, participants develop workable insights into their own leadership styles and how to improve them.
Description_HTML: Examines leadership theories and models. Through intensive readings, exercises, introspection, and open discussion, participants develop workable insights into their own leadership styles and how to improve them.

Subject: LAW
Title: Law and Development
Description: This course explores the relationship between theories of development and legal knowledge. Students tease out the legal theory inherent in development proposals and socio-economic theories informing law & development proposals.
Description_HTML: This course explores the relationship between theories of development and legal knowledge. Students tease out the legal theory inherent in development proposals and socio-economic theories informing law & development proposals.

Subject: LAW
Title: Human Rights
Description: The class leads students through a thorough discussion of foundational issues in international human rights law and activism.
Description_HTML: The class leads students through a thorough discussion of foundational issues in international human rights law and activism.

Subject: LAW
Title: Immigration Law and Policy
Description: Covers statutory, constitutional, and administrative law and policy issues relating to foreign nationals and their relationship with the U.S. government.
Description_HTML: Covers statutory, constitutional, and administrative law and policy issues relating to foreign nationals and their relationship with the U.S. government.

Subject: LAW
Title: Negotiation
Description: Explores how negotiations work, what makes negotiators effective, and why negotiations fail. Focuses on analyzing and improving negotiation skills.
Description_HTML: Explores how negotiations work, what makes negotiators effective, and why negotiations fail. Focuses on analyzing and improving negotiation skills.

Subject: LAW
Title: Law Journals: [Topic]
Description: Welcome to law review! As you already know, working on a law review/journal is very demanding. It is also a unique opportunity in law school to develop your project management skills and work with others.

Welcome to law review! As you already know, working on a law review/journal is very demanding. It is also a unique opportunity in law school to develop your project management skills and work with others.

Subject: LAW
Title: Criminal Defense Clinic
Description: You will represent Defendants in Misdemeanor Criminal Cases, under supervision, including meting clients, reviewing police reports, discussing alternatives and appearing in Circuit Court for Motions, Hearings and possible Jury Trials.
Requisites: Prereq: law Students must be Court Certified so they may appear in Court. Evidence, Professional Responsibility and Constitutional Law are strongly suggested.
Description_HTML:
You will represent Defendants in Misdemeanor Criminal Cases, under supervision, including meting clients, reviewing police reports, discussing alternatives and appearing in Circuit Court for Motions, Hearings and possible Jury Trials.
Requisites: Prereq: law Students must be Court Certified so they may appear in Court. Evidence, Professional Responsibility and Constitutional Law are strongly suggested.

Subject: LAW
Title: Criminal Prosecution Clinic
Description: Students appear in court on behalf of the state; they prepare and argue legal motions and try cases; and they learn Oregon criminal law and procedure.
Requisites: Prereq: Evidence and Legal Profession are required; Trial Practice is recommended.
Description_HTML:
Students appear in court on behalf of the state; they prepare and argue legal motions and try cases; and they learn Oregon criminal law and procedure.
Requisites: Prereq: Evidence and Legal Profession are required; Trial Practice is recommended.

Subject: LAW
Title: Advanced Criminal Prosecution Clinic
Description: Students appear in court on behalf of the state; they prepare and argue legal motions and try cases; and they learn Oregon criminal law and procedure.
Description_HTML:
Students appear in court on behalf of the state; they prepare and argue legal motions and try cases; and they learn Oregon criminal law and procedure.

Subject: LAW
Title: Domestic Violence Civil Clinic I
Description: Students provide necessary legal services to survivors of domestic violence, with an emphasis on family law.
Description_HTML: Students provide necessary legal services to survivors of domestic violence, with an emphasis on family law.

Subject: LAW
Title: Domestic Violence Civil Clinic II
Description: Students provide necessary legal services to survivors of domestic violence, with an emphasis on family law.

Students provide necessary legal services to survivors of domestic violence, with an emphasis on family law.

Subject: LAW
Title: Domestic Violence Protective Order Clinic I
Description: Provides students with the opportunity to effectively serve survivors of domestic violence in protective-order litigation through mock exercises and real-world client representation.
Description_HTML: Provides students with the opportunity to effectively serve survivors of domestic violence in protective-order litigation through mock exercises and real-world client representation.

Subject: LAW
Title: Domestic Violence Protective Order Clinic II
Description: Provides students who have completed the basic clinic with additional opportunities to represent survivors of domestic violence in more complex protective-order proceedings.
Description_HTML:
Provides students who have completed the basic clinic with additional opportunities to represent survivors of domestic violence in more complex protective-order proceedings.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 768.

Subject: LAW
Title: Environmental Law Clinic
Description: Under the supervision of an attorney, students work with non-profit clients in the prosecution of primarily federal environmental cases. Students will join new or existing cases, and conduct legal research and writing to develop memoranda, draft sections of complaints or briefs, and review evidence.
Description_HTML: Under the supervision of an attorney, students work with non-profit clients in the prosecution of primarily federal environmental cases. Students will join new or existing cases, and conduct legal research and writing to develop memoranda, draft sections of complaints or briefs, and review evidence.

Subject: LAW
Title: Advanced Environmental Law Clinic
Description: In the Advanced Environmental Law Clinic, students work one-on-one with attorneys on aspects of an ongoing or prospective case (or cases), with emphasis on research and writing.
Description_HTML:
In the Advanced Environmental Law Clinic, students work one-on-one with attorneys on aspects of an ongoing or prospective case (or cases), with emphasis on research and writing.
Requisites: Prereq: LAW 770.

Subject: LAW
Title: Nonprofit Clinic
Description: In this course students, working in interdisciplinary teams, perform as consultants to several Oregon nonprofit boards of directors. Students will engage with expert consultants, executive directors, and client boards of directors and work to formulate and deliver a meaningful governance assessment to two 501(c)(3) organizations.
Description_HTML: In this course students, working in interdisciplinary teams, perform as consultants to several Oregon nonprofit boards of directors. Students will engage with expert consultants, executive directors, and client boards of directors and work to formulate and deliver a meaningful governance assessment to two 501(c)(3) organizations.

Subject: LAW
Title: LLM Seminar: Writing
Description: Master of laws students will explore the United States legal system and legal profession through in-class workshops, legal research and writing, and oral presentations.
Description_HTML: Master of laws students will explore the United States legal system and legal profession through in-class workshops, legal research and writing, and oral presentations.

Subject: LAW
Title: LLM Seminar: In Practice
Description: Students studying for a master of laws (LLM) degree explore professional development topics and develop practice skills through in-class workshops, legal writing, a simulated symposium, and negotiation exercises.
Description_HTML: Students studying for a master of laws (LLM) degree explore professional development topics and develop practice skills through in-class workshops, legal writing, a simulated symposium, and negotiation exercises.

Subject: LAW
Title: Introduction to American Law and Practice
Description: This class is an introduction to the legal system and the law of the US. Planned specifically for students in the LLM program who are lawyers and legal scholars from countries other than the US, the class aims to provide the context and background knowledge necessary to fully participate in graduate legal education.
Description_HTML: This class is an introduction to the legal system and the law of the US. Planned specifically for students in the LLM program who are lawyers and legal scholars from countries other than the US, the class aims to provide the context and background knowledge necessary to fully participate in graduate legal education.

Subject: LAW
Title: LLM Advanced and Persuasive Legal Writing
Description: This course is specifically designed for international LLM students. The course provides skills in legal writing and research necessary to participate in graduate legal education in the US.
Description_HTML: This course is specifically designed for international LLM students. The course provides skills in legal writing and research necessary to participate in graduate legal education in the US.

Subject: LAW
Title: Comparative Law
Description: Comparative Law offers an introduction to Global Comparative Law, which aims at comparing legal solutions adopted on a regional, international, and transnational level.
Description_HTML: Comparative Law offers an introduction to Global Comparative Law, which aims at comparing legal solutions adopted on a regional, international, and transnational level.

Subject: LAW
Title: American Legal System
Description: American Legal System strives to provide perspective and understanding of the U.S. legal system's basic legal concepts and mechanics. This class will cover topics including, but not limited to, government structure, the judicial system, civil procedure, constitutional law, torts, contracts, criminal law, and procedure.
Description_HTML: American Legal System strives to provide perspective and understanding of the U.S. legal system's basic legal concepts and mechanics. This class will cover topics including, but not limited to, government structure, the judicial system, civil procedure, constitutional law, torts, contracts, criminal law, and procedure.

Subject: LAW
Title: Contemporary Issues in American Indian Law
Description: Provides in-depth study of current issues in American Indian law and US public policy regarding Native Americans.
Description_HTML: Provides in-depth study of current issues in American Indian law and US public policy regarding Native Americans.

Subject: LAW
Title: Comparative Law of Indigenous Peoples
Description: Examines the historical and contemporary legal and policy treatment of indigenous peoples in select countries with significant indigenous populations.
Description_HTML: Examines the historical and contemporary legal and policy treatment of indigenous peoples in select countries with significant indigenous populations.

Subject: LAW
Title: Environmental Law
Description: Overview of environmental law and policy, common-law doctrines, administrative rulemaking, environmental federalism, National Environmental Policy Act, Endangered Species Act, the regulation of hazardous waste, air and water pollution.
Description_HTML: Overview of environmental law and policy, common-law doctrines, administrative rulemaking, environmental federalism, National Environmental Policy Act, Endangered Species Act, the regulation of hazardous waste, air and water pollution.

Subject: LAW
Title: Natural Resources Law
Description: Provides a foundation in environmental law. Spans international, federal, state, and local jurisdiction, interfacing with classic environmental law (pollution statutes).
Description_HTML: Provides a foundation in environmental law. Spans international, federal, state, and local jurisdiction, interfacing with classic environmental law (pollution statutes).

Subject: LAW
Title: Public Trust Law
Description: Explores public trust law, which originated as judge-made law and has been enshrined in many statutes and constitutions in the United States and abroad.
Description_HTML: Explores public trust law, which originated as judge-made law and has been enshrined in many statutes and constitutions in the United States and abroad.

Subject: LAW
Title: Food, Farming, and Sustainability
Description: Examines how laws structure processes of food production, distribution, and consumption; surveys food and agricultural laws in review of broader questions of ecological sustainability and commerce.
Description_HTML: Examines how laws structure processes of food production, distribution, and consumption; surveys food and agricultural laws in review of broader questions of ecological sustainability and commerce.

Title: Energy and the Law Seminar
Description: Introduces students to the policies and laws governing energy in the United States. The class covers federal and state jurisdiction, renewable energy laws and policies, regulation of investor-owned utilities, public power, transmission, and the laws that govern the resources used to generate electricity.

Title: Statistical Methods in Linguistics
Description: This course serves as a hands-on introduction to the state of the art in statistical analysis of linguistic data. Common pitfalls in statistical analysis and the challenges posed by linguistic data are reviewed. Topics covered are mixed-effects regression models, conditional inference trees and model averaging.

Title: Interdisciplinary Cognitive Sciences
Description: This course emphasizes the interdisciplinary study of the human mind through psychology, linguistics, neuroscience, philosophy, and computer science. Students will gain a comprehensive view of cognitive science, practice critical thinking, question theories, and analyze research ideas and assumptions. This course is required for all students completing the Interdisciplinary Cognitive Sciences (ICS) minor.

Title: Advanced Morphology
Description: Learn how words are put together in the world’s languages. This course teaches about the basic building blocks for creating meaning in the languages of the world. It covers topics like prefixes, suffixes, compounding, the profoundly different kinds of grammatical meaning that different languages mark, and how words agree with each other.

Subject: MGMT

Description: Fundamentals of entrepreneurship are applied to solve actual problems for real companies. Students will gain a thorough understanding of project management processes (agile, lean six sigma, sprints, etc.) and learn how to effectively execute a project from inception to final deliverable.

Subject: MGMT
Title: Lean Launchpad Entrepreneurship
Description: The overall goal of the Lean Launchpad Entrepreneurship class is to support students as they learn a methodology for scalable startups in two main areas: customer discovery and customer validation. This means learning not only how to discover and build out your original idea, but also to discover that this initial idea is only a small part of what makes a company successful. This “lean entrepreneurship” methodology will be valuable for the rest of your careers.

Subject: MKTG
Title: Marketing, Sustainability, and Ethics
Description: This course focuses on sustainable marketing, broadly defined. We will define sustainability as any Environmental, Societal, or Governance (ESG) issue that affects the long-term outcomes of consumers, employees, the planet, and/or society. Students in this course will learn to develop sustainable marketing strategies in the process of evaluating firm behavior and predicting consumer and company behaviors that may serve as barrier to sustainable consumption.
Requisites: Prereq: one from MGMT 250, BA 317, MKTG 311.

Subject: MSTC
Title: Fundamentals of Materials in Technology I
Description: Fundamental principles necessary to understand structure-property relations in materials. Key concepts from physics, general chemistry, and mathematics are covered to instill core materials science subject matter and introduce knowledge of challenges in technology requiring innovation in modern materials. Included are thermodynamic concepts of structure from bonding to microstructure, and how to apply these interrelationships between structure and properties in technology relevant materials, diffusion processes, and gain competency in modern programming languages for data analysis. Sequence with MSTC 232.
Description_HTML:
Fundamental principles necessary to understand structure-property relations in materials. Key concepts from physics, general chemistry, and mathematics are covered to instill core materials science subject matter and introduce knowledge of challenges in technology requiring innovation in modern materials. Included are thermodynamic concepts of structure from bonding to microstructure, and how to apply these interrelationships between structure and properties in technology relevant materials, diffusion processes, and gain competency in modern programming languages for data analysis. Sequence with MSTC 232.
Requisites: Coreq: CH 224H, MATH 251Z.

Subject: MSTC
Title: Fundamentals of Materials in Technology II
Description: Continuation of fundamental principles necessary to understand structure-property relations in materials. In this second course (of the two-course sequence), the student will develop a fundamental understanding of defects in solids and how those, along with structure and bonding, govern, mechanical, thermal, electrical, optical, magnetic, and chemical properties of materials used in key technological applications such as computing, energy, and communications, and those used in structural and mechanical engineering. Sequence with MSTC 231.
Description_HTML:
Continuation of fundamental principles necessary to understand structure-property relations in materials. In this second course (of the two-course sequence), the student will develop a fundamental understanding of defects in solids and how those, along with structure and bonding, govern, mechanical, thermal, electrical, optical, magnetic, and chemical properties of materials used in key technological applications such as computing, energy, and communications, and those used in structural and mechanical engineering. Sequence with MSTC 231.
Requisites: Prereq: CH 224H, MATH 251Z, MSTC 231.

Subject: MSTC
Title: Thermal Physics of Advanced Materials
Description: This course is the first one in a two-course sequence that teaches and applies a foundational understanding of thermodynamics, kinetics, and transport to understand how materials are formed and what governs the time evolution of materials processes. This course focuses on the thermodynamic properties of materials, i.e. how concepts of energy minimization determine the equilibrium phases and properties.
Requisites: Prereq: MATH 256; MATH 281; MSTC 232; [ PHYS 351, PHYS 352 ] or [ CH 411, CH 412 ].
Description_HTML:
This course is the first one in a two-course sequence that teaches and applies a foundational understanding of thermodynamics, kinetics, and transport to understand how materials are formed and what governs the time evolution of materials processes. This course focuses on the thermodynamic properties of materials, i.e. how concepts of energy minimization determine the equilibrium phases and properties.
Requisites: Prereq: MATH 256; MATH 281; MSTC 232; [PHYS 351, PHYS 352] or [CH 411, CH 412].

Subject: MSTC
Title: Kinetics and Transport in Advanced Materials
Description: This course is the first one in a two-course sequence that teaches and applies a foundational understanding of thermodynamics, kinetics, and transport to understand how materials are formed and what governs the time evolution of materials processes. Begins with concepts of thermodynamically reversible and irreversible process and how those relate to entropy and entropy production. Transport by diffusion, rates of chemical reactions, and materials processes nucleation and growth and morphological evolution in solids are covered.
Description_HTML:
This course is the first one in a two-course sequence that teaches and applies a foundational understanding of thermodynamics, kinetics, and transport to understand how materials are formed and what governs the time evolution of materials processes. Begins with concepts of thermodynamically reversible and irreversible process and how those relate to entropy and entropy production. Transport by diffusion, rates of chemical reactions, and materials processes nucleation and growth and morphological evolution in solids are covered.
Requisites: Prereq: MSTC 431.

Subject: MSTC
Title: Electronic, Optical and Magnetic Properties of Materials I
Description: In the first part of this two-course sequence, the student will study the significant role played by geometry and chemical composition in determining general material properties and explore how this structure conspires with quantum physics to govern the mechanical, thermal, and electrical properties of condensed matter, including the emergence of semiconducting behavior and superconductivity.
Requisites: Prereq: PHYS 253, MATH 256, MATH 282, MSTC 431; pre- or coreq: MATH 341.
Description_HTML:
In the first part of this two-course sequence, the student will study the significant role played by geometry and chemical composition in determining general material properties and explore how this structure conspires with quantum physics to govern the mechanical, thermal, and electrical properties of condensed matter, including the emergence of semiconducting behavior and superconductivity.
Requisites: Prereq: PHYS 253, MATH 256, MATH 282, MSTC 431; pre- or coreq: MATH 341.

Subject: MSTC
Title: Electronic, Optical and Magnetic Properties of Materials II
Description: In this course, the second part of a sequence, the student will extend their knowledge to magnetic, dielectric, and optical properties, and apply this knowledge to understand semiconductor devices (transistors, photovoltaics, diode lasers, etc.), nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR), spin-based quantum bits, and nanomaterials.
Requisites: Prereq: MSTC 441; pre- or coreq: MATH 341.
Description_HTML:
In this course, the second part of a sequence, the student will extend their knowledge to magnetic, dielectric, and optical properties, and apply this knowledge to understand semiconductor devices (transistors, photovoltaics, diode lasers, etc.), nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR), spin-based quantum bits, and nanomaterials.
Requisites: Prereq: MSTC 441; pre- or coreq: MATH 341.

Title: Quantum Physics

Subject: PHYS
Title: Partial Differential Equations: Fourier Analysis I
Description: Introduction to PDEs with a view towards applications in physics. Wave and heat equations, classical Fourier series on the circle, Bessel and Legendre series. Multilisted with MATH 421M.
Description_HTML:
Introduction to PDEs with a view towards applications in physics. Wave and heat equations, classical Fourier series on the circle, Bessel and Legendre series. Multilisted with MATH 421M.

Equivalent to: MATH 421M

Subject: PHYS
Title: Electromagnetism
Description: Study of electromagnetic waves. Topics include Maxwell's equations, wave equation, plane waves, guided waves, antennas, and other related phenomena.
Description_HTML:
Study of electromagnetic waves. Topics include Maxwell's equations, wave equation, plane waves, guided waves, antennas, and other related phenomena.
Requisites: Prereq: PHYS 413.

Subject: PHYS
Title: Introduction to Space Physics
Description: Course explores the interaction of the solar wind with the Earth's magnetosphere using fundamental plasma physics supported and motivated by spacecraft observations. Students will gain an understanding of the physics governing the interaction building from single particle plasma motion to specific observation supported examples.
Description_HTML:
Course explores the interaction of the solar wind with the Earth's magnetosphere using fundamental plasma physics supported and motivated by spacecraft observations. Students will gain an understanding of the physics governing the interaction building from single particle plasma motion to specific observation supported examples.
Requisites: Prereq: PHYS 253, MATH 282.
Equivalent to: ERTH 423M

Subject: PHYS
Title: Classical Optics
Description: Geometrical optics, polarization, interference, Frauenhofer and Fresnel diffraction.
Description_HTML:
Geometrical optics, polarization, interference, Frauenhofer and Fresnel diffraction.
Requisites: Prereq: PHYS 353.

Subject: PHYS
Title: Modern Optics
Description: Special topics in modern applied optics such as Fourier optics, coherence theory, resonators and lasers, holography, and image processing.
Description_HTML:
Special topics in modern applied optics such as Fourier optics, coherence theory, resonators and lasers, holography, and image processing.
Requisites: Prereq: PHYS 424.

Subject: PHYS
Title: Analog Electronics
Description: Passive and active discrete components and circuits. General circuit concepts and theorems. Equivalent circuits and black box models. Integrated circuit operational amplifiers.
Description_HTML:
Passive and active discrete components and circuits. General circuit concepts and theorems. Equivalent circuits and black box models. Integrated circuit operational amplifiers.
Requisites: Prereq: PHYS 203 or PHYS 253; MATH 256.

Subject: PHYS
Title: Digital Electronics
Description: Digital electronics including digital logic, measurement, signal processing and control. Introduction to computer interfacing.
Description_HTML:
Digital electronics including digital logic, measurement, signal processing and control. Introduction to computer interfacing.
Requisites: Prereq: PHYS 203 or PHYS 253; MATH 253Z.

Subject: PHYS
Title: Radiofrequency and Low-noise Measurements
Description: This course provides a hands-on introduction to radio-frequency technology, essential in communications, physics labs, and quantum computing. Students will learn radio-frequency device design, test equipment, and quantum measurement techniques through lectures and structured labs. In the second half, they will set up and program advanced radio-frequency test instruments used in quantum research, performing real-world measurements.
Description_HTML:
This course provides a hands-on introduction to radio-frequency technology, essential in communications, physics labs, and quantum computing. In the second half, they will set up and program advanced radio-frequency test instruments used in quantum research, performing real-world measurements.
Requisites: Prereq: PHYS 413.

Subject: PHYS
Title: Introduction to Biological Physics
Description: The application of physical principles to the study of the living world, especially at molecular and cellular scales. Topics include the mechanical properties of biomaterials, the sensory abilities of cells and organisms, and the dynamical properties of information processing networks.
Requisites: Prereq: PHYS 353, MATH 281, basic computer programming skills.

Subject: PHYS
Title: Computational Physics
Description: The goal of this course is to build a strong foundation in computational physics. With a particular emphasis on topics in modeling and data analysis for drawing physical insights from data in physics and astronomy, we will cover tools and techniques useful for conducting computationally demanding science.
Description_HTML:
The goal of this course is to build a strong foundation in computational physics. With a particular emphasis on topics in modeling and data analysis for drawing physical insights from data in physics and astronomy, we will cover tools and techniques useful for conducting computationally demanding science.

Subject: PHYS
Title: Design of Experiments
Description: Applies statistics to practical data analysis, data-based decision making, model building, and the design of experiments. Emphasizes factorial designs.
Description_HTML: Applies statistics to practical data analysis, data-based decision making, model building, and the design of experiments. Emphasizes factorial designs.

Subject: PHYS
Title: General Relativity I
Description: This course will serve as an introduction to the concept of gravity as geometry. The course begins by motivating the need for General Relativity and culminate in the presentation of the Einstein equation, presenting some of the simplest solutions to the Einstein equation.
Description_HTML:
This course will serve as an introduction to the concept of gravity as geometry. The course begins by motivating the need for General Relativity and culminate in the presentation of the Einstein equation, presenting some of the simplest solutions to the Einstein equation.
Requisites: Prereq: PHYS 411, PHYS 413.

Subject: PHYS
Title: The Physics Behind Quantum Computers
Description: Quantum computing is a new way of computing based on the rules of Quantum mechanics. We study what sort of computations can be performed by atoms, ions, and superconducting electric circuits.
Description_HTML:
Quantum computing is a new way of computing based on the rules of Quantum mechanics. We study what sort of computations can be performed by atoms, ions, and superconducting electric circuits.
Requisites: Prereq: PHYS 414 is co-req.

Subject: PHYS
Title: Research Project I
Description: For physics and other science majors, Physics Projects entails construction and use of apparatus, interfaces and computers to perform technically-sophisticated experiments, analyze and communicate results.

Subject: PHYS
Title: Nanofabrication
Description: This course introduces students to the design and fabrication of micro- and nanoscale devices. The course will cover fundamental fabrication principles, including optical and electron-beam lithography, thin-film deposition, etching, and characterization. This framework will aid in understanding the processes used in manufacturing chip-based devices in semiconductor electronics, photonics, micro/nano-electromechanical systems, microwave electronics, microfluidics, and superconducting quantum circuits. In the hands-on lab component of this course, students will learn build, optimize and better understand these systems and fabrication processes through the statistical design and analysis of experiments (i.e. Design of Experiments, DOE).

Description_HTML: Thermodynamics, statistical mechanics, kinetic theory, application to gases, liquids, solids, atoms, molecules, and the structure of matter. Sequence.
