Subject: SPED
Title: Foundations in Early Childhood and Early Intervention
Description: Conceptual underpinnings and practical application of an approach to early intervention that links assessment, intervention, and evaluation.
Description_HTML: Conceptual underpinnings and practical application of an approach to early intervention that links assessment, intervention, and evaluation.

Subject: SPED
Title: Early Intervention for Diverse Families
Description: Covers procedures for family assessment, intervention, and evaluation. Addresses adult communication and management strategies.
Description_HTML: Covers procedures for family assessment, intervention, and evaluation. Addresses adult communication and management strategies.

Subject: SPED
Title: Assessment and Evaluation
Description: Presents assessment and evaluation materials used in early intervention programs and provides methods for using these materials.
Description_HTML: Presents assessment and evaluation materials used in early intervention programs and provides methods for using these materials.

Subject: SPED
Title: Curriculum in Early Childhood and Early Intervention
Description: Presents curricular materials covering development from birth to six years. Discusses procedures for use and modification.
Description_HTML: Presents curricular materials covering development from birth to six years. Discusses procedures for use and modification.

Subject: SPED
Title: Autism in Early Intervention
Description: Information is given regarding the etiology of autism, diagnostic/evaluation procedures and current evidence-based strategies for supporting young children with autism spectrum disorder and their families.
Description_HTML: Information is given regarding the etiology of autism, diagnostic/evaluation procedures and current evidence-based strategies for supporting young children with autism spectrum disorder and their families.

Subject: SPED
Title: Early Intervention Methods I
Description: Provide a foundation for understanding the components of a linked system of intervention in Early Intervention/Early Childhood Special Education, where practitioners conduct assessments, plan and implement intervention, assess their intervention, and monitor student progress. Sequence with SPED 688, SPED 689.
Description_HTML: Provide a foundation for understanding the components of a linked system of intervention in Early Intervention/Early Childhood Special Education, where practitioners conduct assessments, plan and implement intervention, assess their intervention, and monitor student progress. Subject: SPED
Title: Early Intervention Methods II
Description: The Early Intervention/Early Childhood Special Education Individualized Family Service Plan, Individualized Education Program Methods II course is the second of three Methods courses to be taken by EI/ECSE students across the year. Sequence with SPED 687, SPED 689.
Description_HTML:
The Early Intervention/Early Childhood Special Education Individualized Family Service Plan, Individualized Education Program Methods II course is the second of three Methods courses to be taken by EI/ECSE students across the year. Sequence with SPED 687, SPED 689.

Subject: SPED
Title: Early Intervention Methods III
Description: Early Intervention/Early Childhood Special Education (EI/ECSE) Methods III is the final course in the EI/ECSE Methods series. It is designed to address specific practices that students will use as professionals in EI/ECSE settings. Sequence with SPED 687, SPED 688.
Description_HTML:
Early Intervention/Early Childhood Special Education (EI/ECSE) Methods III is the final course in the EI/ECSE Methods series. It is designed to address specific practices that students will use as professionals in EI/ECSE settings. Sequence with SPED 687, SPED 688.

Subject: SPED
Title: Professional Practices in Early Intervention I
Description: During this seminar students will focus on the structure and process of teaming within Early Intervention/Early Childhood Special Education systems. Additionally, students will participate in team meetings alongside their peers to reflect on and discuss issues related to their practicum experiences.
Description_HTML:

Title: Professional Practices in Early Intervention II
Description: During this seminar students will focus on Early Intervention/Early Childhood Special Education practices related to transition, intervention planning, implementation of interventions, self-assessment and reflection of practices, and monitoring child progress.

During this seminar students will focus on Early Intervention/Early Childhood Special Education practices related to transition, intervention planning, implementation of interventions, self-assessment and reflection of practices, and monitoring child progress.

Subject: SPED
Title: Professional Practices in Early Intervention III
Description: During this seminar students will focus on Early Intervention/Early Childhood Special Education practices related to student teaching. The purpose of Professional Practices III is to support students in having a positive and productive student teaching experience.

Subject: SPM
Title: Sports Product Branding
Description: Create profitable brand strategies by focusing on how to plan and evaluate brand strategies that create value in the long run.

Subject: SPM
Title: Sports Product Management and Merchandising
Description: Core principles of product creation and merchandising from concept and ideation to delivery of product to retail and consumer. Translate athlete and consumer insights into solutions.
Description_HTML: Core principles of product creation and merchandising from concept and ideation to delivery of product to retail and consumer. Translate athlete and consumer insights into solutions.

Subject: SPM
Title: Human Performance and Sports Products
Description: Exploration of sciences of human performance: physiology and kinesiology, which inform the Sports Product Industry at the level of product development, product design and marketing. Multilisted with HPHY 631M.
Description_HTML:
Exploration of sciences of human performance: physiology and kinesiology, which inform the Sports Product Industry at the level of product development, product design and marketing. Multilisted with HPHY 631M.

Subject: SPM
Title: Sports Product Development
Description: Principles of product development process including new materials research, costing, product prototyping, testing, commercialization, and manufacturing. Identify issues related to product planning and strategy execution.
Description_HTML: Principles of product development process including new materials research, costing, product prototyping, testing, commercialization, and manufacturing. Identify issues related to product planning and strategy execution.

Subject: SPM
Title: Sports Marketing Strategy
Description: Develop the skills to think like a seasoned marketer. Learn factors to consider in the creation and execution of a strategic marketing plan.
Description_HTML: Develop the skills to think like a seasoned marketer. Learn factors to consider in the creation and execution of a strategic marketing plan.

Subject: SPM
Title: Product Creation Lab I
Description: Students will be introduced to the SPM LAB and start to learn the making process for products. The Product Creation Project is built across the complete first year and threads through other program courses. The main objective of the fall term is to practice the fundamentals of product making and develop individual apparel and footwear projects to demonstrate their knowledge. The student’s Capstone project is developed in the Product Creation Lab courses across the complete first year and threads through SPM program courses, resulting in prototypes and sales samples. Sequence with SPM 642, SPM 643.
Description_HTML: Students will be introduced to the SPM LAB and start to learn the making process for products. The Product Creation Project is built across the complete first year and threads through other program courses. The main objective of the fall term is to practice the fundamentals of product making and develop individual apparel and footwear projects to demonstrate their knowledge. The student’s Capstone project is developed in the Product Creation Lab courses across the complete first year and threads through SPM program courses, resulting in prototypes and sales samples. Sequence with SPM 642, SPM 643.

Subject: SPM
Title: Product Creation Lab II
Description: In this course, the second of a 3-course sequence, teams will develop a product design and product development strategy for the team’s integrative project. This will include refining product briefs, defining product priorities, building a 3D prototype, and building a product tech pack. This course will continue to build on the UO SPM Technical Swatch Kit from Fall term. The student’s Capstone project is developed in the Product Creation Lab courses across the complete first year and threads through SPM program courses, resulting in prototypes and sales samples. Sequence with SPM 641, SPM 643.
Description_HTML: In this course, the second of a 3-course sequence, teams will develop a product design and product development strategy for the team’s integrative project. This will include refining product briefs, defining product priorities, building a 3D prototype, and building a product tech pack. This course will continue to build on the UO SPM Technical Swatch Kit from Fall term. The student’s Capstone project is developed in the Product Creation Lab courses across the complete first year and threads through SPM program courses, resulting in prototypes and sales samples. Sequence with SPM 641, SPM 643.

Subject: SPM
Title: Product Creation Lab III
Description: In this final term of a 3 course sequence, teams will continue product design and product development strategy for the team’s integrative project. This will include reﬁning physical products and tech pack revisions to produce actual samples of their products. Sequence with SPM 641, SPM 642.
Description_HTML: In this final term of a 3 course sequence, teams will continue product design and product development strategy for the team’s integrative project. This will include reﬁning physical products and tech pack revisions to produce actual samples of their products. Sequence with SPM 641, SPM 642.

Subject: SPM
Title: Law and Ethics in Sports Product Management
Description: Examines social responsibility and legal concepts in sports management including constitutional regulatory powers, individual participation rights, drug testing, antitrust, labor rights, intellectual property rights, sponsorships, product and event liability. Students may not get credit for both SPM 653 and SBUS 653.
Description_HTML: Examines social responsibility and legal concepts in sports management including constitutional regulatory powers, individual participation rights, drug testing, antitrust, labor rights, intellectual property rights, sponsorships, product and event liability. Students may not get credit for both SPM 653 and SBUS 653.

Subject: SPM
Title: Design Thinking
Description: This course focuses on the transformative power of Design Thinking, a human-centered innovation methodology, for Sports Product Management. Learn to integrate people's needs, technology, and business requirements in problem-solving. Explore its history, philosophy, and hands-on application to various challenges. Gain a valuable skill for future career success.
Description_HTML: This course focuses on the transformative power of Design Thinking, a human-centered innovation methodology, for Sports Product Management. Learn to integrate people's needs, technology, and business requirements in problem-solving. Explore its history, philosophy, and hands-on application to various challenges. Gain a valuable skill for future career success.

Subject: SPM
Title: Consumer Insights
Description: This course offers a methodological overview of consumer research, equipping students with essential skills for effective decision-making in sports product companies and other industries. Learn to define research goals, create measurement instruments, conduct research, analyze data, and present insights for informed business decisions.
Description_HTML: This course offers a methodological overview of consumer research, equipping students with essential skills for effective decision-making in sports product companies and other industries. Learn to define research goals, create measurement instruments, conduct research, analyze data, and present insights for informed business decisions.

Subject: SPM
Title: Sustainability in Sports Products
Description: In this course, students will gain a grasp of sustainability in the apparel and footwear sectors. They'll explore global sustainability definitions, industry standards, and environmental and social impacts. Experts in circularity, materials, and production will offer insights. By course end, students will craft a life cycle analysis framework to predict group product sustainability impacts.
Description_HTML: In this course, students will gain a grasp of sustainability in the apparel and footwear sectors. They'll explore global sustainability definitions, industry standards, and environmental and social impacts. Experts in circularity, materials, and production will offer insights. By course end, students will craft a life cycle analysis framework to predict group product sustainability impacts.

Subject: SPM
Title: Sourcing and Costing Strategies
Description: This course will focus on the theory and management of sourcing and costing decisions under uncertainty in the complex sport and outdoor industry and business environments. Through case studies and real-world applications, students will develop the skills needed to optimize sourcing strategies in a rapidly changing global market.
Description_HTML: This course will focus on the theory and management of sourcing and costing decisions under uncertainty in the complex sport and outdoor industry and business environments. Through case studies and real-world applications, students will develop the skills needed to optimize sourcing strategies in a rapidly changing global market.

Subject: SPM
Title: Digital Connections
Description: This course focuses on the digital landscape of connecting with consumers and covers a range of topics including omni-channel marketing, Artificial Intelligence, and the metaverse. We’ll look at their applications in sports product business and marketing, and workshop how to leverage them effectively to drive growth and innovation. Through three phases - Strategy, Channels, and Future Tech - you'll gain insights into the evolving digital landscape, empowering growth and innovation in sports product business and marketing.
Description_HTML: This course focuses on the digital landscape of connecting with consumers and covers a range of topics including omni-channel marketing, Artificial Intelligence, and the metaverse. We’ll look at their applications in sports product business and marketing, and workshop how to leverage them effectively to drive growth and innovation. Through three phases - Strategy, Channels, and Future Tech - you'll gain insights into the evolving digital landscape, empowering growth and innovation in sports product business and marketing.

Subject: SPM
Title: Sustainable Supply Chain
Description: Sustainable Supply Chain encompasses two important distinct – but ultimately intertwined disciplines: Sustainability and Supply Chain. Students will understand not only how our decisions impact the environment and people involved in making and delivering our products, but also how we can ultimately improve both while still making and delivering amazing products to our consumer. In the end, supply chain is a key part of any business and as such must align to and be in service of our business strategies – consumers, markets, channels, and principles.

In the end, supply chain is a key part of any business and as such must align to and be in service of our business strategies – consumers, markets, channels, and principles.

Subject: SPM
Title: Go To Market
Description: This course focuses on the Go-To-Market (GTM) process in the Outdoor & Sporting Goods industries. Special attention will be paid to the current & future Go-To-Market requirements to be an industry leading brand. We will set a foundation of current Go-To-Market models to understand which components remain relevant and what changes are essential.
Description_HTML: This course focuses on the Go-To-Market (GTM) process in the Outdoor & Sporting Goods industries. Special attention will be paid to the current & future Go-To-Market requirements to be an industry leading brand. We will set a foundation of current Go-To-Market models to understand which components remain relevant and what changes are essential.

Subject: SPM
Title: Sports Product Management Global Trip
Description: The Global Trip is a transformative immersion experience that provides our students with the skill that helps them develop as leaders in a complex and global business world. This global study provides students with the opportunity to visit footwear and apparel manufacturing facilities, brands, and retail marketplaces, and participate in cultural experiences. The primary goals are to understand the cultural and business differences and derive best practices from them. Via this exposure, students can begin to create differentiation. By the end of this trip, students will have a better understanding of the product lifecycle and how global brands operate.

The primary goals are to understand the cultural and business differences and derive best practices from them. Via this exposure, students can begin to create differentiation. By the end of this trip, students will have a better understanding of the product lifecycle and how global brands operate.

Subject: SPM
Title: Leadership I
Description: This course serves as the first step in the Leadership Journey, providing a foundation for leadership development that spans the academic and professional preparation of students in the Sports Product Management (SPM) program. The course introduces essential leadership principles, industry-specific insights, and tools to help students build personalized plans for becoming confident, effective leaders in the dynamic sports and outdoor product industry. Sequence with SPM 695.

The course introduces essential leadership principles, industry-specific insights, and tools to help students build personalized plans for becoming confident, effective leaders in the dynamic sports and outdoor product industry. Sequence with SPM 695.

Subject: SPM
Title: Professional Development I
Description: Sports Product Management Professional Development I is the first course in a three-part series designed to cultivate self-leadership as a foundation for effectively leading and developing others. This course offers opportunities for targeted technical skill enhancement, leadership and teamwork development, and exploration of timely, industry-relevant topics. Through a combination of practical exercises and reflective learning, students will build essential skills to advance their professional growth and impact. Sequence with SPM 692, SPM 693.

This course offers opportunities for targeted technical skill enhancement, leadership and teamwork development, and exploration of timely, industry-relevant topics. Through a combination of practical exercises and reflective learning, students will build essential skills to advance their professional growth and impact. Sequence with SPM 692, SPM 693.

Subject: SPM
Title: Professional Development II
Description: Sports Product Management Professional Development II is the second course in a three-part series designed to support your growth throughout the program and into your career. This course emphasizes developing industry knowledge, cultivating and expanding your professional network, and honing public speaking skills. Students will also continue to enhance technical, leadership, and teamwork abilities while engaging with timely, industry-relevant topics to build a competitive edge. Sequence with SPM 691, SPM 693.

Students will also continue to enhance technical, leadership, and teamwork abilities while engaging with timely, industry-relevant topics to build a competitive edge. Sequence with SPM 691, SPM 693.

Subject: SPM
Title: Professional Development III
Description: Sports Product Management Professional Development III is the final course in a three-part series designed to advance your professional growth and prepare you for leadership roles in the sports and outdoor product industry. This 1-credit, advanced-level independent study builds on the knowledge and skills developed in earlier courses, offering a unique opportunity to explore emerging topics and produce impactful, industry-relevant work. Sequence with SPM 691, SPM 692.

Subject: SPM
Title: Leadership II
Description: As the capstone to the Leadership Journey in the Sport Product Management (SPM) program, this course builds on the foundations of Leadership I and prepares students to lead confidently at all organizational levels. Focused on self-leadership, team leadership, and business leadership, the course equips students with a personalized leadership toolkit through the study of academic and industry best practices, current events, and practical tools. Students will reflect on their leadership behaviors and skills, align their values and motivations with their aspirations, and apply these insights to teamwork experiences. Sequence with SPM 690.

Subject: SPSY
Title: Academic and Behavioral Interventions
Description: The overall goal for this course is for students to demonstrate a solid understanding of the conceptual, theoretical, and empirical bases for school-based, multi-tiered systems of academic and behavior support.

Title: Advanced Consultation
Description: Develops and refines competencies in school-based consultation, and provides opportunities for students to practice consultation skills.
Description_HTML: Develops and refines competencies in school-based consultation, and provides opportunities for students to practice consultation skills.

Subject: SPSY
Title: Developmental Psychopathology
Description: Overview of developmental psychopathology, with emphasis on childhood, adolescence, and early adulthood. Covers phenomenology, etiology, development, classification systems, and prognosis of major psychological disorders.
Description_HTML: Overview of developmental psychopathology, with emphasis on childhood, adolescence, and early adulthood. Covers phenomenology, etiology, development, classification systems, and prognosis of major psychological disorders.

Subject: SPSY
Title: Cognitive-Affective Aspects of Behavior
Description: Examination of cognitive and affective foundations of human behavior. Emphasis on reciprocal influences of cognition and emotion. Selected topics in attention,language, memory, thinking, reasoning. Offered alternate years.
Description_HTML: Examination of cognitive and affective foundations of human behavior. Emphasis on reciprocal influences of cognition and emotion. Selected topics in attention,language, memory, thinking, reasoning. Offered alternate years.

Subject: SPSY
Title: Biological Aspects of Behavior
Description: Provides the appropriate background for students to meet biological psychology course work requirement for board licensure as a psychologist.
Description_HTML: Provides the appropriate background for students to meet biological psychology course work requirement for board licensure as a psychologist.

Subject: SPSY
Title: Principles and Practices in School Psychology
Description: Theory, role, and function of school psychology in its relation to learning and the school setting.
Description_HTML: Theory, role, and function of school psychology in its relation to learning and the school setting.

Subject: SPSY
Title: Foundations of Clinical Supervision
Description: Introduction to theory and practice of clinical supervision. Overview of supervision models, factors affecting the supervision relationship, supervision and evaluation techniques, legal and ethical considerations.
Description_HTML: Introduction to theory and practice of clinical supervision. Overview of supervision models, factors affecting the supervision relationship, supervision and evaluation techniques, legal and ethical considerations.

Subject: SPSY
Title: Professional Ethics
Description: Focuses on the study of current ethical standards of professional practice for school psychologists and behavior analysts.
Description_HTML: Focuses on the study of current ethical standards of professional practice for school psychologists and behavior analysts.

Subject: SPSY
Title: Behavioral Assessment
Description: Principles, techniques, and conceptual and practical issues in behavioral assessment; applied aspects include data gathering and interpretation as well as report writing.
Description_HTML: Principles, techniques, and conceptual and practical issues in behavioral assessment; applied aspects include data gathering and interpretation as well as report writing.

Subject: SPSY
Title: Intellectual Assessment
Description: Covers individual assessment of learning aptitude. Includes administering, scoring, and interpreting intelligence tests as well as report writing. Reviews theories of intelligence.
Description_HTML: Covers individual assessment of learning aptitude. Includes administering, scoring, and interpreting intelligence tests as well as report writing. Reviews theories of intelligence.

Subject: SPSY
Title: Measurement and Assessment
Description: Covers applied knowledge in educational measurement and assessment with an emphasis on use of educational measures for research purposes.
Description_HTML: Covers applied knowledge in educational measurement and assessment with an emphasis on use of educational measures for research purposes.

Subject: SPSY
Title: Educational Assessment
Description: Methods of educational assessment designed to develop and evaluate instructional interventions; topics include systematic observations, curriculum-based assessment, and teacher interviews.

Title: Elementary Statistics I
Description: A first course in statistics focusing on the interpretation and communication of statistical concepts. Introduces exploratory data analysis, descriptive statistics, sampling methods and distributions, point and interval estimates, hypothesis tests for means and proportions, and elements of probability and correlation. Technology will be used when appropriate. Previously MATH 243. Students cannot receive credit for both STAT 243Z and MATH 425.
Requisites: Prereq: MATH 101 or satisfactory placement test score; MATH 111Z recommended; a programmable calculator capable of displaying function graphs.

Subject: WR
Title: Composition I
Description: WR 121Z engages students in the study and practice of critical thinking, reading, and writing. The course focuses on analyzing and composing across varied rhetorical situations and in multiple genres. Students will apply key rhetorical concepts flexibly and collaboratively throughout their writing and inquiry processes. Previously WR 121.
Requisites: Prereq: SAT Reading or SAT Writing score below 37, or ACT verbal score below 32, or equivalent.
Description_HTML:
WR 121Z engages students in the study and practice of critical thinking, reading, and writing. The course focuses on analyzing and composing across varied rhetorical situations and in multiple genres. Students will apply key rhetorical concepts flexibly and collaboratively throughout their writing and inquiry processes. Previously WR 121.
Requisites: Prereq: SAT Reading or SAT Writing score below 37, or ACT verbal score below 32, or equivalent.
Equivalent to: WR 121

Subject: WR
Title: Composition II
Description: WR 122Z builds on concepts and processes emphasized in WR 121Z, engaging with inquiry, research, and argumentation in support of students’ development as writers. The course focuses on composing and revising in research-based genres through the intentional use of rhetorical strategies. Students will find, evaluate, and interpret complex material, including lived experience; use this to frame and pursue their own research questions; and integrate material purposefully into their own compositions. Previously WR 122.
